---
title: "Beta-diversity benchmark — Brito et al. (IL 2016; body site)"
subtitle: "PERMDISP (`vegan::betadisper`) distances; stool vs oral cavity"
author: "Mangiola Laboratory"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 8
    theme: cosmo
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  fig.path: "britoil_2016_paper_figures/"
---

> **Memory requirement:** Rendering this report can require about **40 GB of RAM**.

## Overview

This report generates **paper-ready comparison figures** comparing multiple distance constructions for PERMDISP / `betadisper`:

- **PERMDISP distance inputs**: Bray–Curtis, Aitchison, robust Aitchison, Jaccard, Hellinger

**Simulation settings**: This report uses **Setting 5 from damping_optimization.qmd** (Moderate OD — Treatment Coding — Equal Baseline): σ ≈ 0.011, effect size = 2 for asymmetric DA, effect size = 1 for symmetric DA. The sweep plots (Figure 3) use equal baseline and treatment coding to match this setting.

We intentionally keep the analysis narrow and figure-focused.

## Method comparison

```{r method-comparison-table}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Key properties of distance constructions for PERMDISP / betadisper"

library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Define method categories
methods <- c(
  "PERMDISP distance inputs (Bray–Curtis, Aitchison, etc.)"
)

comparison_df <- data.frame(
  Method = methods,
  `Sensitive to dispersion` = c("Yes"),
  `Sensitive to composition` = c("Yes"),
  `Aware of mean–variance association` = c("No"),
  `Residual-based` = c("No"),
  `Handles zeros` = c("Pseudocount (Aitchison)"),
  `π-aware variance` = c("No"),
  check.names = FALSE
)

# Create table with Nature-style formatting (thick top and bottom borders)
cat('<div style="border-top: 3px solid black; border-bottom: 3px solid black; padding: 10px 0; margin: 20px 0;">')
kable(comparison_df, 
      format = "html",
      align = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
      caption = "Key properties of distance constructions for PERMDISP / betadisper") %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center",
    htmltable_class = "nature-style-table"
  ) %>%
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, align = "c", extra_css = "border-bottom: 1px solid black;") %>%
  column_spec(1, bold = TRUE) %>%
  column_spec(2:7, width = "12em")
cat('</div>')
```

**Key properties:**

- **Sensitive to dispersion**: Detects true differential stochastic overdispersion when present.
- **Sensitive to composition**: Produces false positives when groups differ only in mean composition (undesirable).
- **Aware of mean–variance association**: Accounts for the relationship between mean abundance and variance (log(σ) = -k×μ + c).
- **Residual-based**: Uses residuals (observed - expected) rather than raw counts/proportions.
- **Handles zeros**: Strategy for zero-inflated data (pseudocount, filtering, etc.).
- **π-aware variance**: Uses second-order delta method that explicitly incorporates expected proportions in variance calculations.

## Setup

```{r setup}
#| include: false

# Global filtering threshold will be loaded from targets pipeline

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(vegan)
library(parallelly)
library(patchwork)
library(forcats)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(tidybulk)
library(stringr)
library(ggside)
library(tidyHeatmap)
library(sccomp)
library(ggrepel)
library(lme4)

source("functions.R")

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.path = "britoil_2016_paper_figures/",
  dpi = 220
)

# --- Theme and formatting helpers ---
# Theme for multipanel figures (from https://gist.github.com/stemangiola/fc67b08101df7d550683a5100106561c)
theme_multipanel <-
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0), size = 7),
    axis.title.x = element_text(margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0), size = 7),
    panel.spacing.x = unit(0.1, "lines"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
    strip.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
    strip.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.key.size = unit(5, 'mm'),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
    strip.clip = "off",  # Avoid text clipping for facets
    plot.title = element_text(size = 7),
    axis.line.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
    axis.line.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
    axis.ticks.x = element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
    axis.ticks.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.25)
  )

# Function to drop leading zero from proportions (0.xxx -> .xxx)
dropLeadingZero <- function(l) {
  stringr::str_replace(l, '0(?=\\.)', '')
}

# Helper function to format axis labels: exactly 2 significant digits
# Examples: 25, 2.1, 0.2, 0.05
format_axis_labels <- function(x) {
  # Format to exactly 2 significant digits
  formatted <- signif(x, 2)
  
  # Convert to character with appropriate precision
  result <- character(length(formatted))
  for (i in seq_along(formatted)) {
    val <- formatted[i]
    if (is.na(val)) {
      result[i] <- "NA"
    } else if (val == 0) {
      result[i] <- "0"
    } else {
      val_abs <- abs(val)
      if (val_abs >= 1) {
        # For values >= 1: if integer, show as integer; otherwise 1 decimal
      if (abs(val - round(val)) < 1e-6) {
        result[i] <- as.character(round(val))
      } else {
        result[i] <- sprintf("%.1f", val)
      }
    } else {
        # For values < 1: determine decimal places needed to show exactly 2 significant digits
        # After signif(x, 2), we have a value with 2 significant digits
        # We need to determine how many decimal places are needed to display both digits
        
        # Find the position of the first non-zero digit (most significant)
        first_digit_pos <- floor(log10(val_abs))
        # For 2 significant digits, we need to show digits at positions:
        # first_digit_pos and (first_digit_pos - 1)
        # Number of decimal places needed = -(first_digit_pos - 1) = -first_digit_pos + 1
        # But we need at least enough to show the second digit
        
        # More precise: count how many digits after decimal are needed
        # For 0.25: first digit at -1, second at -2, need 2 decimals
        # For 0.05: first digit at -2, second at -3, need 2 decimals  
        # For 0.2: first digit at -1, but 0.2 rounded to 2 sig digits is 0.20, need 2 decimals
        # Actually, signif(0.2, 2) = 0.2, which has 1 sig digit, so we pad to show 2
        
        # The safest approach: use enough decimals to show 2 significant digits
        # Maximum decimals needed = 2 (for values like 0.05)
        # But we want minimum needed, so:
        # If first digit is at position p, we need to show positions p and p-1
        # Decimal places = max(1, -(p-1)) = max(1, -p+1)
        
        # Actually simpler: for values < 1, we always need at least 1 decimal
        # and at most 2 decimals to show 2 significant digits
        # Let's use a fixed approach: try 2 decimals, then remove trailing zeros
        
        # Use 2 decimals max, then clean up trailing zeros
        result[i] <- sprintf("%.2f", val)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Clean up trailing zeros after decimal point, but preserve standalone zeros
  # Remove trailing zeros: "0.250" -> "0.25", "0.200" -> "0.2", but "0" stays "0"
  result <- stringr::str_replace(result, '(\\.\\d*?)0+$', '\\1')
  # Remove trailing decimal point: "1." -> "1"
  result <- stringr::str_replace(result, '\\.$', '')
  result
}

# --- Paper figure helpers (ggplot + patchwork) ---
plot_betadisper_pcoa_and_boxplot <- function(
  bd,
  title_prefix = "",
  hull = TRUE,
  segments = TRUE,
  label = TRUE,
  cols = c(
    stool = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[10],
    oralcavity = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[2]
  ),
  p_value = NULL,
  p_se = NULL,
  n_perm = NULL,
  show_group_legend = TRUE,
  horizontal_box = FALSE
) {
  # Match vegan::plot.betadisper defaults:
  # - asp = 1
  # - hull = TRUE (convex hull)
  # - segments = TRUE (spider to centroid)
  # - label = TRUE (centroid labels)
  sites <- as.data.frame(vegan::scores(bd, display = "sites"))
  if (ncol(sites) < 2) stop("betadisper site scores have < 2 dimensions.")
  colnames(sites)[1:2] <- c("Axis1", "Axis2")
  sites$group <- factor(bd$group, levels = levels(bd$group))
  # Draw second factor level on top in layered geoms.
  sites <- sites %>% dplyr::arrange(.data$group)

  centroids <- as.data.frame(vegan::scores(bd, display = "centroids"))
  if (ncol(centroids) < 2) stop("betadisper centroid scores have < 2 dimensions.")
  colnames(centroids)[1:2] <- c("Axis1", "Axis2")
  centroids$group <- factor(rownames(centroids), levels = levels(bd$group))
  centroids <- centroids %>% dplyr::arrange(.data$group)

  gl <- levels(sites$group)
  shape_vals <- stats::setNames(rep_len(c(16L, 17L, 15L, 18L), length(gl)), gl)

  seg_df <- NULL
  if (segments) {
    seg_df <- sites %>%
      dplyr::left_join(centroids %>% dplyr::select(group, cx = Axis1, cy = Axis2), by = "group")
  }

  hull_df <- NULL
  if (hull) {
    hull_df <- sites %>%
      dplyr::group_by(group) %>%
      dplyr::group_modify(\(d, ...) {
        if (nrow(d) < 3) return(d[0, , drop = FALSE])
        idx <- grDevices::chull(d$Axis1, d$Axis2)
        d[idx, , drop = FALSE] %>% dplyr::slice(c(seq_len(n()), 1))
      }) %>%
      dplyr::ungroup() %>%
      dplyr::arrange(.data$group)
  }

  p_pcoa <- ggplot() +
    {if (segments) geom_segment(
      data = seg_df,
      aes(x = cx, y = cy, xend = Axis1, yend = Axis2, color = group),
      linewidth = 0.1,
      alpha = 0.5
    )} +
    {if (hull) geom_polygon(
      data = hull_df,
      aes(x = Axis1, y = Axis2, fill = group, group = group, color = group),
      alpha = 0.15,
      linewidth = 0.1
    )} +
    geom_point(
      data = sites,
      aes(x = Axis1, y = Axis2, color = group, shape = group),
      size = 0.1,
      alpha = 0.8,
      stroke = 0
    ) +
    geom_point(
      data = centroids,
      aes(x = Axis1, y = Axis2, color = group),
      shape = 16,
      size = 0.1,
      stroke = 0
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = cols,
      name = NULL,
      guide = if (show_group_legend) "legend" else "none"
    ) +
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = shape_vals,
      name = NULL,
      guide = if (show_group_legend) "legend" else "none"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = cols,
      name = NULL,
      guide = "none"  # Fill is only for polygon shading, not in legend
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "PCoA 1",
      y = "PCoA 2"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
    theme_multipanel +
    theme(aspect.ratio = NULL)  # Allow PCoA plot to resize based on patchwork width

  dist_df <- tibble::tibble(
    group = factor(bd$group, levels = gl),
    distance = unname(bd$distances)
  )

  fmt_p <- function(p) {
    if (!is.finite(p)) return("NA")
    if (p < 0.001) return("<0.001")
    sprintf("%.3f", p)
  }
  ann_label <- NULL
  if (!is.null(p_value)) {
    ann_label <- paste0("p = ", fmt_p(p_value))
    # MC SE and permutation count removed - described in figure caption
  }
  y_max <- max(dist_df$distance, na.rm = TRUE)
  y_annotate <- if (is.finite(y_max)) y_max else NA_real_
  x_min <- min(dist_df$distance, na.rm = TRUE)
  x_rng <- diff(range(dist_df$distance, na.rm = TRUE))
  x_annotate_left <- if (is.finite(x_min) && is.finite(x_rng)) x_min + 0.02 * x_rng else x_min

  if (horizontal_box) {
    # Vertical boxplot: group on x-axis, distance on y-axis (even when horizontal_box=TRUE, use vertical for consistency)
    p_box <- ggplot(dist_df, aes(x = group, y = distance, fill = group)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outlier.size = 0.1, alpha = 0.85, linewidth = 0.25, outlier.stroke = 0) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 1.0, alpha = 0.25, stroke = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cols, guide = "none") +
      labs(x = NULL, y = "Distance to centroid") +
      {if (!is.null(ann_label)) annotate(
        geom = "text",
        x = 1.5,
        y = Inf,
        label = ann_label,
        size = 3.0,
        fontface = "plain",
        vjust = 1.1
      )} +
      coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
      theme_multipanel +
      theme(
        aspect.ratio = NULL  # Allow boxplot to resize based on patchwork width
      )
  } else {
    # Vertical boxplot: group on x-axis, distance on y-axis
    p_box <- ggplot(dist_df, aes(x = group, y = distance, fill = group)) +
      geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outlier.size = 0.1, alpha = 0.85, linewidth = 0.25, outlier.stroke = 0) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.12, size = 1.0, alpha = 0.25, stroke = 0) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cols, guide = "none") +
      labs(x = NULL, y = "Distance to centroid") +
      {if (!is.null(ann_label)) annotate(
        geom = "text",
        x = 1.5,
        y = Inf,
        label = ann_label,
        size = 3.0,
        fontface = "plain",
        vjust = 1.1
      )} +
      coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
      theme_multipanel +
      theme(
        aspect.ratio = NULL  # Allow boxplot to resize based on patchwork width
      )
  }

  list(pcoa = p_pcoa, box = p_box)
}

# Express centroid separation as a percentage of average group hull radius (PCoA 1-2).
# 100% means a centroid shift equal to the average hull radius.
compute_centroid_distance_pct_hull_radius <- function(bd) {
  sites <- as.data.frame(vegan::scores(bd, display = "sites"))
  if (ncol(sites) < 2) {
    return(list(
      centroid_dist_pcoa12 = NA_real_,
      mean_hull_radius = NA_real_,
      pct_of_mean_hull_radius = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  colnames(sites)[1:2] <- c("Axis1", "Axis2")
  sites$group <- bd$group

  centroids <- as.data.frame(vegan::scores(bd, display = "centroids"))
  if (nrow(centroids) != 2 || ncol(centroids) < 2) {
    return(list(
      centroid_dist_pcoa12 = NA_real_,
      mean_hull_radius = NA_real_,
      pct_of_mean_hull_radius = NA_real_
    ))
  }
  colnames(centroids)[1:2] <- c("Axis1", "Axis2")
  centroid_dist <- sqrt(sum((centroids[1, 1:2] - centroids[2, 1:2])^2))

  hull_radii <- sites %>%
    dplyr::group_by(group) %>%
    dplyr::group_map(\(d, ...) {
      if (nrow(d) < 3) return(NA_real_)
      cx <- mean(d$Axis1, na.rm = TRUE)
      cy <- mean(d$Axis2, na.rm = TRUE)
      radial_dist <- sqrt((d$Axis1 - cx)^2 + (d$Axis2 - cy)^2)
      max(radial_dist, na.rm = TRUE)
    }) %>%
    unlist(use.names = FALSE)

  mean_hull_radius <- mean(hull_radii, na.rm = TRUE)
  pct <- if (is.finite(mean_hull_radius) && mean_hull_radius > 0) {
    100 * centroid_dist / mean_hull_radius
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }

  list(
    centroid_dist_pcoa12 = centroid_dist,
    mean_hull_radius = mean_hull_radius,
    pct_of_mean_hull_radius = pct
  )
}

# Format analysis results for plotting - takes output from run_analysis_job(return_full = TRUE)
# This function formats analysis results into structures needed for plotting (stats, betadisper objects, distances)
format_analysis_results_for_plotting <- function(
  analysis_results,
  case_label,
  standard_distance_methods = c("bray", "aitchison", "robust.aitchison", "jaccard", "hellinger"),
  group_levels = c("stool", "oralcavity")
) {

  if (!is.list(analysis_results) || !"method_results" %in% names(analysis_results)) {
    stop("analysis_results must be output from run_analysis_job(return_full = TRUE)")
  }

  method_results <- analysis_results$method_results
  method_names <- names(method_results)
  
  # Validate that we have the expected methods
  if (!is.character(standard_distance_methods) || length(standard_distance_methods) < 1) {
    stop("standard_distance_methods must be a non-empty character vector.")
  }
  if (anyDuplicated(standard_distance_methods)) {
    stop("standard_distance_methods contains duplicates.")
  }

  # Format statistics
  stats <- purrr::imap_dfr(method_results, \(mr, mn) {
    tibble::tibble(
      case = case_label,
      method = mn,
      group1 = mr$group1,
      group2 = mr$group2,
      delta = mr$delta,
      p_value = mr$p_value,
      p_se = mr$p_se,
      n_perm = mr$n_perm
    )
  }) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(method = factor(.data$method, levels = method_names))

  # Format distances for boxplots
  distances_long <- purrr::imap_dfr(method_results, \(mr, mn) {
    tibble::tibble(
      sample_id = names(mr$distances),
      group = mr$group,
      distance = mr$distances,
      method = mn
    )
  }) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      method = factor(.data$method, levels = method_names),
      group = factor(.data$group, levels = group_levels)
    )

  # Extract betadisper objects
  bd_by_method <- purrr::imap(method_results, \(mr, mn) mr$bd)
  
  # Return formatted results
  list(
    stats = stats,
    bd_by_method = bd_by_method,
    bd = list(
      standard = method_results[[paste0("permdisp_", standard_distance_methods[[1]])]]$bd
    ),
    distances_long = distances_long
  )
}

plot_alpha_diversity_scatter <- function(sim_result, case_label = "Case") {
  # This is the same style used in the discovery report:
  # `print(alpha_1$plot)` from `run_alpha_diversity_analysis()`.
  # BritoIL: two body sites (hardcoded for this QMD only).
  alpha <- run_alpha_diversity_analysis(
    sim_result,
    case_label = case_label,
    group_levels = c("stool", "oralcavity"),
    group_colors = c(
      stool = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[10],
      oralcavity = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[2]
    )
  )
  color_vals <- c(
    stool = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[10],
    oralcavity = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[2]
  )
  # Update plot to use theme_multipanel and remove titles/subtitles
  # Note: stat_ellipse linewidth is set in functions.R
  # Add marginal densities using ggside (fill will inherit from color scale if overridden)
  # Override point size to match boxplots (run_alpha_diversity_analysis uses size = 0.1)
  alpha$plot +
    labs(title = NULL, subtitle = NULL) +
    theme_multipanel +
    ggplot2::geom_point(ggplot2::aes(x = richness, y = pielou_evenness, color = group), 
                        size = 1.0, alpha = 0.6, stroke = 0,
                        position = ggplot2::position_jitter(width = 0.25, height = 0.003),
                        inherit.aes = FALSE, data = alpha$data) +
    ggside::geom_xsidedensity(aes(x = richness, fill = group), alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.25) +
    ggside::geom_ysidedensity(aes(y = pielou_evenness, fill = group), alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.25) +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = color_vals, guide = "none") +
    ggplot2::theme(
      ggside.axis.ticks.x.bottom = ggplot2::element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
      ggside.axis.ticks.y.left = ggplot2::element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
      ggside.axis.line.x.bottom = ggplot2::element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
      ggside.axis.line.y.left = ggplot2::element_line(linewidth = 0.25),
      ggside.panel.scale.x = 0.15,
      ggside.panel.scale.y = 0.15
    )
}

# Whisker plot: 95% intervals of proportions per taxon and group,
# with taxa ordered by overall mean abundance (descending).
plot_mean_dispersion_scatter <- function(sim_result, case_label = "Case", cols = NULL) {
  if (is.null(cols)) {
    cols <- c(
      stool = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[10],
      oralcavity = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[2]
    )
  }

  d <- sim_result$count_long %>%
    dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / library_size) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(group, taxon_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      mean_prop = mean(proportion, na.rm = TRUE),
      p_low = stats::quantile(proportion, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
      p_high = stats::quantile(proportion, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  taxon_order <- d %>%
    dplyr::group_by(taxon_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(overall_mean = mean(mean_prop, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(overall_mean)) %>%
    dplyr::pull(taxon_id)

  d <- d %>%
    dplyr::mutate(taxon_id = factor(taxon_id, levels = taxon_order))

  ggplot(d, aes(x = taxon_id, y = mean_prop, ymin = p_low, ymax = p_high, color = group)) +
    ggplot2::geom_linerange(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.6), linewidth = 0.25, alpha = 0.8) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 0.8, stroke = 0) +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = NULL) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) dropLeadingZero(scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)(x))) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Taxon (ordered by mean proportion)",
      y = "Proportion (95% interval)"
    ) +
    theme_multipanel +
    ggplot2::theme(
      axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = ggplot2::element_blank()
    )
}

# Distance input helpers live in `_targets_BritoIL_2016.R` (analysis only).

# Method label functions
pretty_method_label <- function(method_key) {
  if (startsWith(method_key, "permdisp_")) {
    dm <- sub("^permdisp_", "", method_key)
    if (dm == "bray") return("Bray–Curtis")
    if (dm == "aitchison") return("Aitchison")
    if (dm == "robust.aitchison") return("robust Aitchison")
    if (dm == "jaccard") return("Jaccard")
    if (dm == "hellinger") return("Hellinger")
    return(dm)
  }
  method_key
}

pretty_method_label_roc <- function(method_key) {
  # Short labels for ROC plots
  if (startsWith(method_key, "permdisp_")) {
    dm <- sub("^permdisp_", "", method_key)
    if (dm == "bray") return("Bray–Curtis")
    if (dm == "aitchison") return("Aitchison")
    if (dm == "robust.aitchison") return("robust Aitchison")
    if (dm == "jaccard") return("Jaccard")
    if (dm == "hellinger") return("Hellinger")
    return(dm)
  }
  method_key
}

pretty_method_label_box <- function(method_key) {
  # Labels for boxplots (same as pretty_method_label)
  pretty_method_label(method_key)
}

# Define consistent color scheme for all methods (used across all plots)
roc_colors_all <- c(
  "Bray–Curtis" = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Set1")[1],
  "Aitchison" = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Set1")[2],
  "robust Aitchison" = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Set1")[3],
  "Jaccard" = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Set1")[4],
  "Hellinger" = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Set1")[5]
)
method_levels <- names(roc_colors_all)

# Single source of truth for method levels/colors used in all sweeps and summaries.
coerce_method_levels <- function(x) {
  x_chr <- trimws(as.character(x))
  unknown <- setdiff(unique(x_chr), method_levels)
  if (length(unknown) > 0) {
    stop("Unknown method labels (no color mapping defined): ", paste(unknown, collapse = ", "))
  }
  factor(x_chr, levels = method_levels)
}

# format_analysis_results_for_plotting is defined in this QMD (plotting helper function)
```

```{r brito-sccomp-input}
#| cache: false

# BritoIL_2016 sccomp fit derived from curatedMetagenomicData.
# Factor of interest for this benchmark: body_site (stool vs oralcavity).
# Following mia/OMA-style preprocessing, we keep target body sites and apply
# prevalence+detection filtering before model fitting.
sccomp_file_path <- "BritoIL_2016_sccomp_results.rds"

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(curatedMetagenomicData)
  library(TreeSummarizedExperiment)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(sccomp)
  library(tidySummarizedExperiment)
  library(magrittr)
})

tse <- curatedMetagenomicData(
  "BritoIL_2016.relative_abundance",
  dryrun = FALSE,
  counts = TRUE
) %$%
 `2021-03-31.BritoIL_2016.relative_abundance` |>
  dplyr::filter(body_site %in% c("stool", "oralcavity")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(body_site = factor(body_site, levels = c("stool", "oralcavity")))

# MIA-style feature filtering:
# - detection threshold: at least 10 counts to call a taxon "present"
# - prevalence threshold: present in at least 10% of samples

n_taxa_before <- nrow(tse)
tse <- mia::subsetByPrevalent(
  tse,
  detection = 10L,
  prevalence = 0.10,
  assay.type = "relative_abundance"
)
n_taxa_after <- nrow(tse)

cat("BritoIL_2016 sccomp input prepared.\n")
cat("Samples:", n_distinct(tse$.sample), "\n")
cat("Taxa:", n_distinct(tse$.feature), "\n")
cat("Taxa before/after prevalence filter:", n_taxa_before, "/", n_taxa_after, "\n")
print(
  tse |>
    dplyr::distinct(.sample, body_site) |>
    dplyr::count(body_site)
)
```

```{r fit-sccomp-brito-if-needed}
#| cache: false

if (!file.exists(sccomp_file_path)) {
  message("Running sccomp_estimate() because file is missing: ", sccomp_file_path)
  fit <- mia::meltSE(
    tse,
    assay.type = "relative_abundance",
    add.row = FALSE,
    add.col = TRUE
  ) |>

  sccomp::sccomp_estimate(
    formula_composition = ~ body_site + subject_id + age_category + gender + DNA_extraction_kit,
    formula_variability = ~ body_site,
    sample = "SampleID",
    cell_group = "FeatureID",
    abundance = "relative_abundance",
    inference_method = "hmc",
    cores = max(1, parallelly::availableCores() - 1), 
    bimodal_mean_variability_association = TRUE,
    verbose = TRUE, 
    max_sampling_iterations = 3000,
    sig_figs = 6, 
    portable = FALSE,

    # TEST
    exclude_priors = TRUE
  ) |>
    sccomp_test(contrasts = c("Intercept" = "`(Intercept)`", "body_siteoralcavity"))
  
  fit = fit |> 
    sccomp:::incorporate_parameters_into_sccomp_object(
    parameters_to_load = c( 
      "prec_intercept_1",
      "prec_slope_1",
      "prec_intercept_2",
      "prec_slope_2",
      "prec_sd",
      "mix_p",
      "beta",
      "alpha"
    ))
  
  saveRDS(fit, sccomp_file_path)
  message("Saved sccomp fit to: ", sccomp_file_path)
} else {
  message("Using existing sccomp fit: ", sccomp_file_path)
}

# Always load the fit object for downstream QC/targets chunks.
fit <- readRDS(sccomp_file_path)
```

## QC plots
```{r qc-plot}
fit |>
  sccomp_test(contrasts = "body_siteoralcavity") |>
  sccomp_plot_intervals_2D(factor = "body_site")
```


```{r targets-run}
#| eval: true

library(targets)
library(VGAM)

targets::tar_script({

library(targets)
library(tarchetypes)
library(crew)
library(crew.cluster)

# Local controller for Mac development
local_controller <- crew_controller_local(
  name = "local",
  workers = 8, #parallelly::availableCores(),
  seconds_idle = 30
)

# SLURM controller for HPC/Linux
# Helper (optional) to avoid repetition
new_elastic <- function(name, mem_gb, time_min, workers, crashes_max, backup = NULL) {
  crew_controller_slurm(
    name = name,
    workers = workers,
    crashes_max = crashes_max,
    seconds_idle = 30,
    options_cluster = crew_options_slurm(
      memory_gigabytes_required = mem_gb,
      cpus_per_task = 2,
      time_minutes = time_min
    ),
    backup = backup
  )
}

# Small → large, with fallbacks to the next size up
elastic_160 <- new_elastic("elastic_160", 160, 60 * 24, workers = 8,  crashes_max = 2)
elastic_80  <- new_elastic("elastic_80",   80,  60 * 4,  workers = 16, crashes_max = 1, backup = elastic_160)
elastic_40  <- new_elastic("elastic_40",   40,  60 * 4,  workers = 24, crashes_max = 1, backup = elastic_80)
elastic_20  <- new_elastic("elastic_20",   20,  60 * 4,  workers = 32, crashes_max = 1, backup = elastic_40)
elastic_10  <- new_elastic("elastic_10",   10,  60 * 4,  workers = 48, crashes_max = 1, backup = elastic_20)
elastic_5   <- new_elastic("elastic_5",     5, 60 * 4,  workers = 200, crashes_max = 6, backup = elastic_10)

# Group for targets (small → large)
slurm_controller <- crew_controller_group(
  elastic_5, elastic_10, elastic_20, elastic_40, elastic_80, elastic_160
)

# Select controller based on OS
controller <- if (Sys.info()["sysname"] == "Darwin") {
  local_controller  # macOS
} else {
  slurm_controller   # Linux/HPC
}

tar_option_set(
  memory = "transient",
  garbage_collection = 100,
  storage = "worker",
  retrieval = "worker",
  # error = "continue",
  workspace_on_error = TRUE,
  format = "rds",
  controller = controller,
  packages = c(
    "dplyr", "tidyr", "purrr", "tibble", "stringr",
    "ggplot2", "vegan", "parallelly",
    "sccomp", "patchwork"
  )
)

#!/usr/bin/env R

# Helper functions for {targets} pipeline.
# These mirror the parameter extraction logic in the Quarto report.

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(purrr)
  library(tibble)
  library(vegan)
})

source("functions.R")

utils::globalVariables(c(
  "parameter", "c_effect", "c_lower", "c_upper", "v_effect",
  "sample_id", "taxon_id", "count", "library_size", "mean_prop",
  "group", "n_da_taxa", "method", "p_value", "n_fp", "n_sims",
  "simulate_compositional_bb"
))

load_sccomp_result <- function(path) {
  readRDS(path)
}

list(
  tar_target(
    sccomp_result_file,
    "BritoIL_2016_sccomp_results.rds",
    format = "file"
  ),
  # Simulation/analysis helpers live in functions.R; track as a file target so changing the
  # sim algorithm invalidates downstream targets (source() alone is invisible to {targets}).
  tar_target(
    functions_r,
    "functions.R",
    format = "file"
  ),
  tar_target(
    sccomp_result,
    load_sccomp_result(sccomp_result_file)
  ),
  tar_target(
    sccomp_params,
    {
      functions_r
      # Dispersion for Brito sims: taxon intercepts from v_effect (log-sigma mapping), not prec_intercept.
      # DA/DA_sd sweeps use mean_dispersion_assoc_slope = 0; keep k_real = 0 so DO paths match.
      extract_sccomp_params_brito(sccomp_result)
    }
  ),
  # Number of simulation replications (set to small number for development)
  tar_target(
    n_reps_auc,
    ifelse(Sys.info()["sysname"] == "Darwin", 1L, 100L)  # Change this to 20 or higher for final runs
  ),
  tar_target(
    abundance_thresholds,
    c(0.0, 0.005, 0.001)  # 0.0 = unfiltered, 0.005 = 0.5%, 0.001 = 0.1% 
  ),
  tar_target(
    sim_params,
    build_simulation_params_brito(
      sccomp_params,
      n_reps_auc = n_reps_auc,
      group_levels = c("stool", "oralcavity")
    )
  ),
  # Setting DA_no_sd: Simulation targets (differential abundance, no sd on overdispersion)
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs,
    {
      jobs <- tidyr::crossing(seed = sim_params$rep_seeds_auc, n_da_taxa = 0:40)
      jobs$branch_index <- seq_len(nrow(jobs))
      jobs$target_name <- "sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim"
      jobs
    }, packages = c("VGAM")
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim,
    run_simulation_job_brito(
      sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs,
      dispersion_intercept_by_taxon = sim_params$intercept_disp_realistic_taxon,
      composition_intercept_by_taxon = sim_params$mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic,
      mean_dispersion_assoc_slope = 0,
      slope_effects_distribution = sccomp_params$slope_effects_significant,
      sd_log_overdispersion = 0,
      n_taxa = sim_params$n_taxa,
      n_samples = sim_params$n_samples,
      n_samples_per_group = sim_params$n_samples_per_group,
      library_size_mean = sim_params$library_size_mean,
      library_size_sd = sim_params$library_size_sd
    ) |> synthetic_sim_add_two_group_factor(c("stool", "oralcavity")),
    pattern = map(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs),
    iteration = "list"
  ),
  # Setting DA_no_sd: Analysis targets (with thresholds including unfiltered)
  # Create jobs grid: sim results × thresholds (0.0 = unfiltered, 0.005, 0.001)
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold,
    {
      sim_jobs <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs
      jobs <- tidyr::crossing(
        seed = sim_jobs$seed,
        n_da_taxa = sim_jobs$n_da_taxa,
        threshold = abundance_thresholds
      )
      jobs$branch_index <- seq_len(nrow(jobs))
      jobs
    }
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim_with_threshold,
    run_simulation_job_brito(
      sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold,
      dispersion_intercept_by_taxon = sim_params$intercept_disp_realistic_taxon,
      composition_intercept_by_taxon = sim_params$mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic,
      mean_dispersion_assoc_slope = 0,
      slope_effects_distribution = sccomp_params$slope_effects_significant,
      sd_log_overdispersion = 0,
      n_taxa = sim_params$n_taxa,
      n_samples = sim_params$n_samples,
      n_samples_per_group = sim_params$n_samples_per_group,
      library_size_mean = sim_params$library_size_mean,
      library_size_sd = sim_params$library_size_sd
    ) |> synthetic_sim_add_two_group_factor(c("stool", "oralcavity")),
    pattern = map(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold),
    iteration = "list"
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res,
    {
      threshold <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold$threshold
      
      # Apply filtering (threshold = 0.0 keeps all taxa, effectively unfiltered)
      sim_filtered <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim_with_threshold
      sim_filtered$count_long <- filter_by_abundance_cases(
        sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim_with_threshold$count_long,
        threshold = threshold
      )
      result <- run_analysis_job(sim_r = sim_filtered, return_full = TRUE)
      
      # Add threshold to the result for later filtering/plotting
      result$threshold <- threshold
      result$seed <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim_with_threshold$seed
      result$n_da_taxa <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold$n_da_taxa
      result
    },
    pattern = map(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold, sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim_with_threshold),
    iteration = "list"
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_fpr,
    summarise_fpr(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res)
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_res,
    {
      threshold <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold$threshold
      seed <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim_with_threshold$seed
      n_da_taxa <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold$n_da_taxa

      run_permanova_job(
        sim_r = sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim_with_threshold,
        threshold = threshold,
        seed = seed,
        n_da_taxa = n_da_taxa,
        permutations = 199
      )
    },
    pattern = map(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold, sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim_with_threshold),
    iteration = "list"
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_tpr,
    summarise_permanova_tpr(dplyr::bind_rows(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_res))
  ),
  # Setting DA_sd: Simulation targets (differential abundance, with sd on overdispersion)
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs,
    {
      jobs <- tidyr::crossing(seed = sim_params$rep_seeds_auc, n_da_taxa = 0:40)
      jobs$branch_index <- seq_len(nrow(jobs))
      jobs$target_name <- "sweep_setting_DA_sd_sim"
      jobs
    }, packages = c("VGAM")
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_sd_sim,
    run_simulation_job_brito(
      sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs,
      dispersion_intercept_by_taxon = sim_params$intercept_disp_realistic_taxon,
      composition_intercept_by_taxon = sim_params$mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic,
      mean_dispersion_assoc_slope = 0,
      slope_effects_distribution = sccomp_params$slope_effects_significant,
      sd_log_overdispersion = sim_params$sd_log_overdispersion_realistic,
      n_taxa = sim_params$n_taxa,
      n_samples = sim_params$n_samples,
      n_samples_per_group = sim_params$n_samples_per_group,
      library_size_mean = sim_params$library_size_mean,
      library_size_sd = sim_params$library_size_sd
    ) |> synthetic_sim_add_two_group_factor(c("stool", "oralcavity")),
    pattern = map(sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs),
    iteration = "list"
  ),
  # Setting DA_sd: Analysis targets (with thresholds including unfiltered)
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs_with_threshold,
    {
      sim_jobs <- sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs
      jobs <- tidyr::crossing(
        seed = sim_jobs$seed,
        n_da_taxa = sim_jobs$n_da_taxa,
        threshold = abundance_thresholds
      )
      jobs$branch_index <- seq_len(nrow(jobs))
      jobs
    }
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_sd_sim_with_threshold,
    run_simulation_job_brito(
      sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs_with_threshold,
      dispersion_intercept_by_taxon = sim_params$intercept_disp_realistic_taxon,
      composition_intercept_by_taxon = sim_params$mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic,
      mean_dispersion_assoc_slope = 0,
      slope_effects_distribution = sccomp_params$slope_effects_significant,
      sd_log_overdispersion = sim_params$sd_log_overdispersion_realistic,
      n_taxa = sim_params$n_taxa,
      n_samples = sim_params$n_samples,
      n_samples_per_group = sim_params$n_samples_per_group,
      library_size_mean = sim_params$library_size_mean,
      library_size_sd = sim_params$library_size_sd
    ) |> synthetic_sim_add_two_group_factor(c("stool", "oralcavity")),
    pattern = map(sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs_with_threshold),
    iteration = "list"
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_sd_res,
    {
      threshold <- sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs_with_threshold$threshold

      # Apply filtering (threshold = 0.0 keeps all taxa, effectively unfiltered)
      sim_filtered <- sweep_setting_DA_sd_sim_with_threshold
      sim_filtered$count_long <- filter_by_abundance_cases(
        sweep_setting_DA_sd_sim_with_threshold$count_long,
        threshold = threshold
      )
      result <- run_analysis_job(sim_r = sim_filtered, return_full = TRUE)
      
      result$threshold <- threshold
      result$seed <- sweep_setting_DA_sd_sim_with_threshold$seed
      result$n_da_taxa <- sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs_with_threshold$n_da_taxa
      result
    },
    pattern = map(sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs_with_threshold, sweep_setting_DA_sd_sim_with_threshold),
    iteration = "list"
  ),
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DA_sd_fpr,
    summarise_fpr(sweep_setting_DA_sd_res)
  ),

  # Setting DO_no_sd: Define jobs (differential overdispersion, no sd, no DA)
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_jobs,
    {
      set.seed(105)
      n_reps_roc <- sim_params$n_reps_auc
      rep_seeds <- 21000 + seq_len(n_reps_roc)
      delta_grid <- c(0, 0.05, 0.10, 0.15, 0.20, 0.25, 0.375, 0.5, 0.625, 0.75, 0.875, 1.0)
      jobs <- tidyr::crossing(seed = rep_seeds, delta = delta_grid, threshold = abundance_thresholds)
      jobs$branch_index <- seq_len(nrow(jobs))
      jobs
    }
  ),
  
  # Setting DO_no_sd: Run each job (simulation + analysis)
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_res,
    {
      intercept_base <- -6
      perm_reps_roc <- 199
      methods <- c("bray", "aitchison", "robust.aitchison", "jaccard", "hellinger")
      
      seed <- sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_jobs$seed
      delta <- sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_jobs$delta
      threshold <- sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_jobs$threshold
      intercept_disp_cohort <- c(intercept_base, intercept_base + delta)
      intercept_disp_matrix <- outer(intercept_disp_cohort, rep(1, sim_params$n_taxa))

      sim_r <- simulate_case_realistic_taxa(
        slope_vector = rep(0, sim_params$n_taxa),
        mu_inv_softmax = sim_params$mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic,
        log_dispersion_assoc = 0,
        intercept_dispersion_matrix = intercept_disp_matrix,
        sd_log_overdispersion = 0,
        seed = seed,
        n_taxa = sim_params$n_taxa,
        n_samples_per_group = sim_params$n_samples_per_group,
        library_size_mean = sim_params$library_size_mean,
        library_size_sd = sim_params$library_size_sd
      ) |> synthetic_sim_add_two_group_factor(c("stool", "oralcavity"))

      # Apply filtering (threshold = 0.0 keeps all taxa, effectively unfiltered)
      sim_r$count_long <- filter_by_abundance_cases(sim_r$count_long, threshold = threshold)
      n_taxa_after_filter <- length(unique(sim_r$count_long$taxon_id))
      if (n_taxa_after_filter < 3) {
        return(tibble::tibble(
          seed = integer(0),
          delta = numeric(0),
          truth = logical(0),
          method = character(0),
          p_value = numeric(0),
          centroid_dist = numeric(0),
          centroid_sep_pcoa12 = numeric(0),
          mean_hull_radius = numeric(0),
          centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius = numeric(0)
        ))
      }

      inputs_std <- purrr::set_names(
        lapply(methods, \(dm) {
          build_standard_betadisper_inputs_paper(
            count_long = sim_r$count_long,
            sample_metadata = sim_r$sample_metadata,
            distance_method = dm,
            group_levels = c("stool", "oralcavity")
          )
        }),
        nm = paste0("permdisp_", methods)
      )

      purrr::imap_dfr(inputs_std, \(inp, method_key) {
        bd <- betadisper(inp$dist, inp$group)
        p <- permutest_betadisper_fixed(bd, permutations = perm_reps_roc)$p_value

        dist_df <- data.frame(distance = bd$distances, group = bd$group)
        group_means <- tapply(dist_df$distance, dist_df$group, mean)
        centroid_distance <- abs(group_means["oralcavity"] - group_means["stool"])

        # Centroid separation in PCoA 1-2, scaled by average group hull radius.
        sites <- as.data.frame(vegan::scores(bd, display = "sites"))
        centroids <- as.data.frame(vegan::scores(bd, display = "centroids"))
        centroid_sep_pcoa12 <- NA_real_
        mean_hull_radius <- NA_real_
        centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius <- NA_real_
        if (ncol(sites) >= 2 && nrow(centroids) == 2 && ncol(centroids) >= 2) {
          colnames(sites)[1:2] <- c("Axis1", "Axis2")
          sites$group <- bd$group
          colnames(centroids)[1:2] <- c("Axis1", "Axis2")

          centroid_sep_pcoa12 <- sqrt(sum((centroids[1, 1:2] - centroids[2, 1:2])^2))
          hull_radii <- sites %>%
            dplyr::group_by(group) %>%
            dplyr::summarise(
              hull_radius = {
                cx <- mean(Axis1, na.rm = TRUE)
                cy <- mean(Axis2, na.rm = TRUE)
                max(sqrt((Axis1 - cx)^2 + (Axis2 - cy)^2), na.rm = TRUE)
              },
              .groups = "drop"
            )
          mean_hull_radius <- mean(hull_radii$hull_radius, na.rm = TRUE)
          if (is.finite(mean_hull_radius) && mean_hull_radius > 0) {
            centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius <- 100 * centroid_sep_pcoa12 / mean_hull_radius
          }
        }

        tibble::tibble(
          seed = seed,
          delta = delta,
          threshold = threshold,
          truth = delta > 0,
          method = pretty_method_label_roc(method_key),
          p_value = p,
          centroid_dist = as.numeric(centroid_distance),
          centroid_sep_pcoa12 = as.numeric(centroid_sep_pcoa12),
          mean_hull_radius = as.numeric(mean_hull_radius),
          centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius = as.numeric(centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius)
        )
      })
    },
    pattern = map(sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_jobs)
  ),
  
  # Setting DO_no_sd: Aggregate results
  tar_target(
    sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_fpr,
    {
      dplyr::bind_rows(sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_res) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(score = -log10(pmax(p_value, 1e-300))) %>%
        dplyr::filter(method != "Arcsin resid.")
    }
  )
)


}, script = "_targets_BritoIL_2016.R", ask=FALSE)

# Run the targets pipeline
targets::tar_make(script = "_targets_BritoIL_2016.R", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
```

```{r targets-load}
# Set up targets configuration and load basic parameters used throughout
# Individual chunks will load their specific targets as needed
targets_script <- file.path(getwd(), "_targets_BritoIL_2016.R")
if (!file.exists(targets_script)) {
  qmd_dir <- dirname(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE))
  targets_script <- file.path(qmd_dir, "_targets_BritoIL_2016.R")
}
targets::tar_config_set(script = targets_script)
# Source helper functions from _targets_BritoIL_2016.R AFTER tar_script/tar_make.
source(targets_script)

# Load basic parameters that are used in many places
sccomp_params <- targets::tar_read("sccomp_params", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
sim_params <- targets::tar_read("sim_params", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
abundance_thresholds <- targets::tar_read("abundance_thresholds", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
# Default threshold for regular plots (use 0.005)
abundance_threshold <- 0.005

# Extract commonly used parameters
n_samples_real <- sccomp_params$n_samples_real
n_taxa_real <- sccomp_params$n_taxa_real
k_real <- sccomp_params$k_real
k_sd_real <- sccomp_params$k_sd_real
c_real <- sccomp_params$c_real
prec_sd_real <- sccomp_params$prec_sd_real
intercept_effects <- sccomp_params$intercept_effects
slope_effects <- sccomp_params$slope_effects
slope_effects_significant <- sccomp_params$slope_effects_significant
v_intercept <- sccomp_params$v_intercept
v_slope <- sccomp_params$v_slope

n_taxa <- sim_params$n_taxa
n_samples_per_group <- sim_params$n_samples_per_group
n_groups <- sim_params$n_groups
n_samples <- sim_params$n_samples
group_levels <- sim_params$group_levels
library_size_mean <- sim_params$library_size_mean
library_size_sd <- sim_params$library_size_sd
mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic <- sim_params$mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic
intercept_disp_realistic <- sim_params$intercept_disp_realistic
sigma_realistic <- sim_params$sigma_realistic
slope_realistic <- sim_params$slope_realistic
n_reps_auc <- sim_params$n_reps_auc
perm_reps_auc <- sim_params$perm_reps_auc
rep_seeds_auc <- sim_params$rep_seeds_auc
```


## Shared simulation settings

Simulation parameters and helper functions are now defined in the targets pipeline
(`_targets_BritoIL_2016.R`) and loaded via the `targets-load` chunk.



# Data Properties

## Goal

Use **realistic simulation parameters extracted from BritoIL_2016 microbiome data** (sccomp fit) to evaluate the performance of various beta-diversity methods under conditions that mirror real microbiome datasets.

This approach ensures our simulation sweeps reflect:

1. **Real sample and taxa counts** from BritoIL_2016
2. **Real mean-overdispersion association (k)** estimated from the data
3. **Real variability around the association (σ)**
4. **Real baseline abundance distribution** (intercept effects)
5. **Real slope distribution** (group effects)

## Extract Parameters from BritoIL_2016 sccomp Fit

```{r load-sccomp-results, cache: false}
# Load the sccomp results
result <- readRDS(sccomp_file_path)

# Identify the non-intercept coefficient name dynamically
# (e.g. groupIBD, body_siteoralcavity, etc.).
group_parameter <- result |>
  dplyr::filter(.data$parameter != "Intercept") |>
  dplyr::distinct(.data$parameter) |>
  dplyr::pull(.data$parameter) |>
  (\(x) x[[1]])()

if (is.na(group_parameter) || is.null(group_parameter)) {
  stop("Could not identify non-intercept group parameter in sccomp result.")
}

# Extract fit object
fit <- attr(result, "fit")

# Get basic dataset dimensions from the result
# The sccomp result stores dimensions in model_input (a list)
model_input <- attr(result, "model_input")

# Extract N (samples) and M (taxa/cell groups) from model_input list
if (!is.null(model_input) && is.list(model_input)) {
  n_samples_real <- model_input$N
  n_taxa_real <- model_input$M
} else {
  # Fallback: use the cell_group column in result (unique taxa)
  n_taxa_real <- length(unique(result$cell_group))
  # Samples: from the sccomp print output
  n_samples_real <- 178
}

cat("=== BritoIL_2016 Dataset Characteristics ===\n")
cat("Number of samples:", n_samples_real, "\n")
cat("Number of taxa (genera):", n_taxa_real, "\n")
```

```{r extract-dispersion-params}
# Extract mean-overdispersion association coefficient (k)
# from sccomp precision parameterization (`prec_intercept_j` / `prec_slope_j`, or unindexed names).
precision_params <- extract_precision_trend_params(fit)
prec_intercept <- precision_params$prec_intercept
prec_slope <- precision_params$prec_slope

# Direct mapping to simulator parameters:
# k = prec_slope (the simulator formula has -k, so negative prec_slope gives positive effect)
k_real <- prec_slope  # ≈ -0.64
k_sd_real <- precision_params$k_sd_real

# c = -prec_intercept (convert from precision to overdispersion intercept)
c_real <- -prec_intercept  # ≈ -5.16

cat("\n=== Precision Parameters from sccomp ===\n")
cat("Precision intercept:", round(prec_intercept, 4), "\n")
cat("Precision slope (", precision_params$slope_parameter, "): ", round(prec_slope, 4), "\n", sep = "")

cat("\n=== Converted to Overdispersion (sigma) Parameters ===\n")
cat("k (log_dispersion_assoc = prec_slope):", round(k_real, 4), "\n")
cat("c (intercept_dispersion = -prec_intercept):", round(c_real, 4), "\n")
cat("SD of k:", round(k_sd_real, 4), "\n")

cat("\n=== Interpretation ===\n")
cat("With k =", round(k_real, 2), "and formula log(σ) = -k*log(μ) + c:\n")
cat("  -k =", round(-k_real, 2), ", so higher mean → higher σ → MORE overdispersion ✓\n")

# Extract precision SD (variability around the mean-od relationship)
prec_sd_summary <- fit$summary("prec_sd")
prec_sd_real <- prec_sd_summary$mean[1]

cat("\n=== Precision SD (variability around k) ===\n")
cat("Estimated prec_sd:", round(prec_sd_real, 4), "\n")
cat("SD:", round(prec_sd_summary$sd[1], 4), "\n")
```

```{r extract-baseline-params}
# Extract baseline abundance distribution (intercept effects for composition)
intercept_effects <- result %>%
  filter(parameter == "Intercept") %>%
  pull(c_effect)

cat("\n=== Baseline Abundance Distribution (Intercept c_effect) ===\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(intercept_effects), 4), "\n")
cat("SD:", round(sd(intercept_effects), 4), "\n")
cat("Range: [", round(min(intercept_effects), 4), ",", 
    round(max(intercept_effects), 4), "]\n")

# Plot intercept distribution
intercept_mean <- mean(intercept_effects)
p_intercept <- ggplot(data.frame(c_effect = intercept_effects), aes(x = c_effect)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgrey", alpha = 1, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = intercept_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_rug() +
  labs(x = "inv. softmax proportion", y = "Density") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  theme_multipanel
```

```{r extract-slope-params}
# Extract slope (group effect) distribution - ALL taxa
slope_effects <- result %>%
  filter(parameter == group_parameter) %>%
  pull(c_effect)

# Extract SIGNIFICANT slope effects (credible interval excludes 0)
# sccomp results have c_lower and c_upper columns for credible intervals
slope_data <- result %>%
  filter(parameter == group_parameter)

# Check if credible interval columns exist
if ("c_lower" %in% names(slope_data) && "c_upper" %in% names(slope_data)) {
  # Significant = credible interval does not include 0
  slope_effects_significant <- slope_data %>%
    filter(c_lower > 0 | c_upper < 0) %>%
    pull(c_effect)
} else {
  # Fallback: use effect size threshold (|effect| > 0.5 as "significant")
  slope_effects_significant <- slope_data %>%
    filter(abs(c_effect) > 0.5) %>%
    pull(c_effect)
}

cat("\n=== Group Effect Distribution (Slope c_effect for IBD) ===\n")
cat("All taxa:\n")
cat("  Mean:", round(mean(slope_effects), 4), "\n")
cat("  SD:", round(sd(slope_effects), 4), "\n")
cat("  Range: [", round(min(slope_effects), 4), ",", 
    round(max(slope_effects), 4), "]\n")
cat("  N taxa:", length(slope_effects), "\n")

cat("\nSignificant taxa only (CI excludes 0 or |effect| > 0.5):\n")
cat("  N significant:", length(slope_effects_significant), "\n")
if (length(slope_effects_significant) > 0) {
  cat("  Mean:", round(mean(slope_effects_significant), 4), "\n")
  cat("  SD:", round(sd(slope_effects_significant), 4), "\n")
  cat("  Range: [", round(min(slope_effects_significant), 4), ",", 
      round(max(slope_effects_significant), 4), "]\n")
}

# Plot slope distribution - all taxa
slope_mean <- mean(slope_effects)
p_slope <- ggplot(data.frame(c_effect = slope_effects), aes(x = c_effect)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgrey", alpha = 1, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = slope_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_rug(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "inv. softmax proportion", y = "Density") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  theme_multipanel
```

```{r extract-variability-params}
# Extract variability parameters (v_effect for intercept and slope)
# These are the per-taxon variability effects in sccomp
v_intercept <- result %>%
  filter(parameter == "Intercept") %>%
  pull(v_effect)

v_slope <- result %>%
  filter(parameter == group_parameter) %>%
  pull(v_effect)

cat("\n=== Variability Parameters (v_effect) ===\n")
cat("Intercept v_effect - Mean:", round(mean(v_intercept), 4), 
    ", SD:", round(sd(v_intercept), 4), "\n")
cat("Slope v_effect - Mean:", round(mean(v_slope), 4), 
    ", SD:", round(sd(v_slope), 4), "\n")

# Use c_real from precision conversion (c = -prec_intercept)
# This is the proper intercept for our simulator's overdispersion parameterization
intercept_dispersion_real <- c_real  # From extract-dispersion-params: c_real = -prec_intercept ≈ -5.16
sigma_real <- exp(intercept_dispersion_real)

cat("\n=== Simulation Intercept Dispersion (from precision conversion) ===\n")
cat("intercept_dispersion (c = -prec_intercept):", round(intercept_dispersion_real, 4), "\n")
cat("sigma (exp(intercept_dispersion)):", format(sigma_real, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3), "\n")
cat("\nNote: This is very low overdispersion (σ ≈ 0.006), consistent with high precision.\n")
```

```{r additional-parameter-plots}
#| eval: !expr Sys.info()[["sysname"]] != "Darwin"
# CmdStan draw CSVs are not available locally on macOS (absolute paths from Linux cluster).

# 1. Intercept c_effect in softmax scale (proportions)
# Convert intercept effects to proportions using inv_softmax
# We need to convert all intercept effects together to get proper proportions
intercept_softmax <- inv_softmax(intercept_effects)

arcsin_sqrt_trans_prop <- scales::trans_new(
  name = "arcsin_sqrt",
  transform = function(x) asin(sqrt(pmax(0, pmin(1, x)))),
  inverse = function(y) sin(y)^2,
  breaks = scales::extended_breaks(),
  domain = c(0, 1)
)

cat("\n=== Intercept c_effect in Softmax Scale (Proportions) ===\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(intercept_softmax), 4), "\n")
cat("SD:", round(sd(intercept_softmax), 4), "\n")
cat("Range: [", round(min(intercept_softmax), 4), ",", 
    round(max(intercept_softmax), 4), "]\n")

intercept_softmax_mean <- mean(intercept_softmax)
p_intercept_softmax <- ggplot(data.frame(proportion = intercept_softmax), aes(x = proportion)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 1, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = intercept_softmax_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_rug() +
  labs(x = "Proportion", y = "Density") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  theme_multipanel

# 2. Effect in proportion fold change using sccomp_proportional_fold_change
prop_fold_change_data <- sccomp_proportional_fold_change(
  result,
  formula_composition = ~ body_site,
  from = "stool",
  to = "oralcavity"
)

prop_fold_change <- prop_fold_change_data[["proportion_fold_change"]]
if (is.null(prop_fold_change)) {
  pfc_cols <- names(prop_fold_change_data)
  stop(
    "sccomp_proportional_fold_change did not return a 'proportion_fold_change' column. ",
    "Columns: ", paste(pfc_cols, collapse = ", ")
  )
}

feature_col <- dplyr::case_when(
  "cell_group" %in% names(prop_fold_change_data) ~ "cell_group",
  ".feature" %in% names(prop_fold_change_data) ~ ".feature",
  "FeatureID" %in% names(prop_fold_change_data) ~ "FeatureID",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
if (is.na(feature_col)) {
  stop(
    "Could not find a feature column in proportional fold-change output. ",
    "Columns: ", paste(names(prop_fold_change_data), collapse = ", ")
  )
}

prop_fold_change_tbl <- prop_fold_change_data %>%
  dplyr::transmute(cell_group = .data[[feature_col]], proportion_fold_change = .data$proportion_fold_change)

result_feature_col <- dplyr::case_when(
  "cell_group" %in% names(result) ~ "cell_group",
  ".feature" %in% names(result) ~ ".feature",
  "FeatureID" %in% names(result) ~ "FeatureID",
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)
if (is.na(result_feature_col)) {
  stop(
    "Could not find a feature column in sccomp `result`. ",
    "Columns: ", paste(names(result), collapse = ", ")
  )
}

significant_taxa <- result %>%
  dplyr::filter(parameter == group_parameter) %>%
  dplyr::filter(c_lower > 0 | c_upper < 0) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(cell_group = .data[[result_feature_col]])

prop_fold_change_sig <- prop_fold_change_tbl %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(significant_taxa, by = "cell_group") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(abs_fold_change = abs(proportion_fold_change))

cat("\n=== Absolute Proportion Fold Change (significant body_site effects) ===\n")
cat("N significant taxa:", nrow(prop_fold_change_sig), "\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(prop_fold_change_sig$abs_fold_change, na.rm = TRUE), 4), "\n")
cat("SD:", round(sd(prop_fold_change_sig$abs_fold_change, na.rm = TRUE), 4), "\n")
cat("Range: [", round(min(prop_fold_change_sig$abs_fold_change, na.rm = TRUE), 4), ",", 
    round(max(prop_fold_change_sig$abs_fold_change, na.rm = TRUE), 4), "]\n")

prop_fold_change_mean <- mean(prop_fold_change_sig$abs_fold_change, na.rm = TRUE)
p_prop_fold_change <- ggplot(prop_fold_change_sig, aes(x = abs_fold_change)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 1, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = prop_fold_change_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_rug() +
  labs(x = "Absolute proportional fold change", y = "Density") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  theme_multipanel

# 3. Distribution of v_effect (variability) - split by intercept and slope
cat("\n=== Intercept v_effect Distribution ===\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(v_intercept), 4), "\n")
cat("SD:", round(sd(v_intercept), 4), "\n")
cat("Range: [", round(min(v_intercept), 4), ",", 
    round(max(v_intercept), 4), "]\n")

# Convert v_effect using simulator mapping (log(sigma) = v_effect for Brito targets).
log_overdispersion_intercept <- v_intercept
log_overdispersion_slope <- v_slope

cat("\n=== Log Overdispersion Intercept (converted from v_effect) ===\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(log_overdispersion_intercept), 4), "\n")
cat("SD:", round(sd(log_overdispersion_intercept), 4), "\n")
cat("Range: [", round(min(log_overdispersion_intercept), 4), ",", 
    round(max(log_overdispersion_intercept), 4), "]\n")

log_overdispersion_intercept_mean <- mean(log_overdispersion_intercept)
p_v_effect_intercept <- ggplot(data.frame(log_overdispersion = log_overdispersion_intercept), aes(x = log_overdispersion)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgrey", alpha = 1, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = log_overdispersion_intercept_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_rug(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "log overdispersion", y = "Density") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  theme_multipanel

cat("\n=== Log Overdispersion Slope (converted from v_effect) ===\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(log_overdispersion_slope), 4), "\n")
cat("SD:", round(sd(log_overdispersion_slope), 4), "\n")
cat("Range: [", round(min(log_overdispersion_slope), 4), ",", 
    round(max(log_overdispersion_slope), 4), "]\n")

log_overdispersion_slope_mean <- mean(log_overdispersion_slope)
p_v_effect_slope <- ggplot(data.frame(log_overdispersion = log_overdispersion_slope), aes(x = log_overdispersion)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgrey", alpha = 1, color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = log_overdispersion_slope_mean, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_rug(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(x = "log overdispersion", y = "Density") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels) +
  theme_multipanel
```

```{r combined-parameter-plot, fig.width=16, fig.height=12}
#| eval: !expr Sys.info()[["sysname"]] != "Darwin"

# Combined parameter visualization
# One row: intercept c_ and proportion, slope c_ and proportional fold, then OD intercept and slope
# Remove y-axis labels, numbers, and ticks from all plots except the leftmost one
# Also reduce side margins to minimize spacing between panels
# Add titles on top of each distribution
# Add small margins between panels (leftmost has no left margin, rightmost has no right margin)
p_intercept_adj <- p_intercept + 
  ggtitle("Intercept c_effect") +
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 2, 0, 0, "pt"), plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5))
p_intercept_softmax_no_y <- p_intercept_softmax + 
  ggtitle("Proportion") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 2, 0, 2, "pt"), plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5))
p_slope_no_y <- p_slope + 
  ggtitle("Slope c_effect") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 2, 0, 2, "pt"), plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5))
p_prop_fold_change_no_y <- p_prop_fold_change + 
  ggtitle("Proportional fold change") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 2, 0, 2, "pt"), plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5))
p_v_effect_intercept_no_y <- p_v_effect_intercept + 
  ggtitle("OD intercept") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 2, 0, 2, "pt"), plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5))
p_v_effect_slope_no_y <- p_v_effect_slope + 
  ggtitle("OD slope") +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
        plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 2, "pt"), plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5))

p_combined <- p_intercept_adj | p_intercept_softmax_no_y | p_slope_no_y | p_prop_fold_change_no_y | p_v_effect_intercept_no_y | p_v_effect_slope_no_y
p_combined <- p_combined + 
  theme(legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'))

p_combined
```

```{r combined-parameter-plot-save-pdf}
#| echo: false
#| eval: !expr Sys.info()[["sysname"]] != "Darwin"
ggsave("Figure_1_BritoIL_2016.pdf", p_combined, width = 183, height = 183/4, units = "mm")
```

## Simulation Setup with Realistic Parameters

```{r simulation-params}
set.seed(123)

# Match sccomp fit: full taxon count (analyses use all taxa that pass abundance filters)
n_taxa <- n_taxa_real
n_samples_per_group <- floor(n_samples_real / 2)  # Split into 2 groups
n_groups <- 2
n_samples <- n_samples_per_group * n_groups
group_levels <- c("stool", "oralcavity")  # Matches simulation output (body_site)

library_size_mean <- 15125  # From HMP2 average total counts per sample
library_size_sd <- 5000

cat("=== Simulation Parameters ===\n")
cat("n_taxa:", n_taxa, "(from fitted model)\n")
cat("n_samples_per_group:", n_samples_per_group, "\n")
cat("Total n_samples:", n_samples, "\n")

# Use real baseline distribution - sample from fitted intercepts
# Scale to n_taxa and center
set.seed(123)
sampled_intercepts <- sample(intercept_effects, n_taxa, replace = TRUE)
mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic <- sampled_intercepts - mean(sampled_intercepts)

cat("\nRealistic baseline (sampled from HMP2 intercepts):\n")
cat("Range: [", round(min(mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic), 3), ",", 
    round(max(mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic), 3), "]\n")

# Use real k value for mean-overdispersion association
k_realistic <- k_real

# Use real intercept dispersion for overdispersion level
intercept_disp_realistic <- intercept_dispersion_real
sigma_realistic <- sigma_real

cat("\nRealistic overdispersion parameters:\n")
cat("k (mean-OD association):", round(k_realistic, 4), "\n")
cat("intercept_dispersion:", round(intercept_disp_realistic, 4), "\n")
cat("sigma:", round(sigma_realistic, 6), "\n")

# Create realistic slope vector by directly sampling from the real distribution
# This captures the natural pattern of group effects observed in HMP2
set.seed(123)
slope_realistic <- sample(slope_effects, n_taxa, replace = TRUE)
# Center to sum to zero (compositional constraint)
slope_realistic <- slope_realistic - mean(slope_realistic)

# Verify lengths
stopifnot(length(mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic) == n_taxa)
stopifnot(length(slope_realistic) == n_taxa)

cat("\nRealistic slope vector (sampled from HMP2 group effects):\n")
cat("Mean:", round(mean(slope_realistic), 4), "\n")
cat("SD:", round(sd(slope_realistic), 4), "\n")
cat("Range: [", round(min(slope_realistic), 3), ",", round(max(slope_realistic), 3), "]\n")

# Sweep parameters
n_reps_auc <- 20  # Number of replicates
perm_reps_auc <- 199
rep_seeds_auc <- 12000 + seq_len(n_reps_auc)
```

Design matrix helpers are now defined in `_targets_BritoIL_2016.R`.

Helper functions are now defined in `_targets_BritoIL_2016.R`.

```{r simulation-function}
# Simulation functions for sweeps now live in the targets pipeline (`_targets_BritoIL_2016.R`).
# Function to create composite plot for DA taxa sweep
plot_da_taxa_sweep_results <- function(
  fpr_data,
  setting_name,
  sigma_val,
  intercept_disp_val,
  x_limits = NULL,
  x_breaks = NULL
) {
  standard_methods_labels <- method_levels

  # Convert raw method keys to pretty labels, then restrict to the 5 standard methods
  fpr_data_with_labels <- fpr_data %>%
    dplyr::mutate(method_label = dplyr::case_when(
      method == "permdisp_bray" ~ "Bray–Curtis",
      method == "permdisp_aitchison" ~ "Aitchison",
      method == "permdisp_robust.aitchison" ~ "robust Aitchison",
      method == "permdisp_robust_aitchison" ~ "robust Aitchison",
      method == "permdisp_jaccard" ~ "Jaccard",
      method == "permdisp_hellinger" ~ "Hellinger",
      as.character(method) %in% standard_methods_labels ~ as.character(method),
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(method_label)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(method_label = coerce_method_levels(method_label))
  
  # Handle both uppercase FPR and lowercase fpr column names
  fpr_col <- if ("FPR" %in% names(fpr_data_with_labels)) "FPR" else "fpr"
  
  # Add threshold handling if threshold column exists
  has_threshold <- "threshold" %in% names(fpr_data_with_labels) && 
                   !all(is.na(fpr_data_with_labels$threshold))
  
  if (has_threshold) {
    fpr_data_with_labels <- fpr_data_with_labels %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        threshold_label = dplyr::case_when(
          threshold == 0.0 ~ "unfiltered",
          threshold == 0.005 ~ "0.5%",
          threshold == 0.001 ~ "0.1%",
          TRUE ~ as.character(threshold)
        ),
        alpha_val = dplyr::case_when(
          threshold == 0.0 ~ 0.25,
          threshold == 0.005 ~ 1.0,
          threshold == 0.001 ~ 0.55,
          TRUE ~ 1.0
        )
      )
  } else {
    fpr_data_with_labels$alpha_val <- 1.0
  }
  
  p <- ggplot(fpr_data_with_labels, aes(x = n_da_taxa, y = !!sym(fpr_col), color = method_label, alpha = alpha_val)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.1) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.1, aes(group = if(has_threshold) interaction(method_label, threshold) else method_label)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.0, stroke = 0, aes(group = if(has_threshold) interaction(method_label, threshold) else method_label)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = roc_colors_all, breaks = standard_methods_labels, drop = FALSE, name = NULL) +
    scale_alpha_identity(guide = "none") +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = if (is.null(x_breaks)) unique(fpr_data_with_labels$n_da_taxa) else x_breaks,
      limits = x_limits,
      labels = format_axis_labels
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels, limits = c(0, 1)) +
    labs(
      x = "Number of DA taxa",
      y = "False positive rate"
    ) +
    theme_multipanel
  
  p
}
```

```{r plotting-function}
# Function to create PCoA plot with facet-grid-like styling
plot_pcoa_single_method <- function(analysis_result, method_key, n_da_label, 
                                        is_first_col = FALSE, is_first_row = FALSE, 
                                        is_last_row = FALSE, is_last_col = FALSE,
                                        method_label = NULL) {
  if (is.null(analysis_result) || is.null(analysis_result$bd_by_method)) return(NULL)
  bd <- analysis_result$bd_by_method[[method_key]]
  if (is.null(bd)) return(NULL)

  p_val <- analysis_result$stats %>%
    dplyr::filter(method == method_key) %>%
    dplyr::pull(p_value)
  if (length(p_val) == 0) p_val <- NA_real_

  if (is.null(method_label)) {
    method_label <- pretty_method_label_box(method_key)
  }

  gg <- plot_betadisper_pcoa_and_boxplot(
    bd,
    title_prefix = "",
    cols = cols_prgn_setting5,
    p_value = p_val,
    show_group_legend = FALSE,
    horizontal_box = FALSE
  )
  
  # Customize PCoA plot
  p <- gg$pcoa
  
  # Add column title (n DA) only on first row
  if (is_first_row) {
    p <- p + ggtitle(n_da_label) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 4)))
  } else {
    p <- p + theme(plot.title = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Add method label annotation on the left side (like a facet strip)
  if (is_first_col) {
    # Get actual data range from betadisper scores for proper positioning
    sites_scores <- as.data.frame(vegan::scores(bd, display = "sites"))
    if (ncol(sites_scores) >= 2) {
      x_range <- range(sites_scores[[1]], na.rm = TRUE)
      y_range <- range(sites_scores[[2]], na.rm = TRUE)
      x_center <- mean(x_range)
      y_center <- mean(y_range)
      # Position label to the left of the plot, centered vertically
      # Use a position that's clearly outside the data range
      label_x <- x_range[1] - 0.15 * diff(x_range)
    } else {
      # Fallback if we can't get scores
      label_x <- -Inf
      y_center <- 0
    }
    
    p <- p + 
      # Add label positioned in left margin area, centered vertically
      annotate(
        "text",
        x = label_x,
        y = y_center,
        label = method_label,
        angle = 90,
        hjust = 0.5,  # Center horizontally when rotated
        vjust = 0.5,
        size = 2.8,
        fontface = "bold"
      ) +
      # Adjust left margin to make room for rotated label
      theme(plot.margin = margin(l = 55, r = 0, t = 0, b = 0, "pt")) +
      coord_cartesian(clip = "off")
  } else {
    # No margins for non-first columns
    p <- p + theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"))
  }
  
  # Show y-axis title only on leftmost column
  if (!is_first_col) {
    p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Show x-axis title only on bottom row
  if (!is_last_row) {
    p <- p + theme(axis.title.x = element_blank())
  }
  
  p
}

# Function to create PCoA grid with facet-grid-like styling
plot_pcoa_grid_multiple_methods <- function(analysis_list, n_da_labels) {
  standard_distance_methods_pcoa <- c("bray", "aitchison", "robust.aitchison", "jaccard", "hellinger")
  methods <- paste0("permdisp_", standard_distance_methods_pcoa)
  n_methods <- length(methods)
  n_cols <- length(analysis_list)
  
  all_plots <- list()
  
  # Iterate by row (method) first, then column (n_da) to match byrow = TRUE layout
  for (method_idx in seq_along(methods)) {
    method <- methods[method_idx]
    method_label <- pretty_method_label_box(method)
    is_first_row <- (method_idx == 1)
    is_last_row <- (method_idx == n_methods)
    
    for (col_idx in seq_along(analysis_list)) {
      analysis_result <- analysis_list[[col_idx]]
      n_da_label <- n_da_labels[col_idx]
      is_first_col <- (col_idx == 1)
      is_last_col <- (col_idx == n_cols)
      
      p <- plot_pcoa_single_method(
        analysis_result = analysis_result,
        method_key = method,
        n_da_label = n_da_label,
        is_first_col = is_first_col,
        is_first_row = is_first_row,
        is_last_row = is_last_row,
        is_last_col = is_last_col,
        method_label = method_label
      )
      
      if (!is.null(p)) {
        all_plots[[length(all_plots) + 1]] <- p
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Arrange in grid: n_methods rows, n_cols columns
  # byrow = TRUE means fill row by row, which matches our iteration order
  patchwork::wrap_plots(all_plots, nrow = n_methods, ncol = n_cols, byrow = TRUE) +
    patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
}
```

```{r sweep-selection-helpers}
get_sweep_index <- function(jobs, seed, n_da_taxa) {
  idx <- which(jobs$seed == seed & jobs$n_da_taxa == n_da_taxa)
  if (length(idx) != 1) {
    stop("Expected exactly one sweep job for seed=", seed, " n_da_taxa=", n_da_taxa)
  }
  idx
}

get_sweep_index_with_threshold <- function(jobs_with_threshold, seed, n_da_taxa, threshold) {
  idx <- which(jobs_with_threshold$seed == seed & 
               jobs_with_threshold$n_da_taxa == n_da_taxa & 
               jobs_with_threshold$threshold == threshold)
  if (length(idx) != 1) {
    stop("Expected exactly one sweep job for seed=", seed, " n_da_taxa=", n_da_taxa, " threshold=", threshold)
  }
  idx
}

get_sweep_analysis_with_threshold <- function(sweep_res, jobs_with_threshold, seed, n_da_taxa, threshold) {
  sweep_res[[get_sweep_index_with_threshold(jobs_with_threshold, seed, n_da_taxa, threshold)]]
}
```

# Simulation no-DA (No Differential Abundance)

This section tests method performance when there is **differential overdispersion but NO differential abundance**. This evaluates whether methods correctly detect differential dispersion without producing false positives from composition differences.

All simulations in this section use realistic BritoIL_2016-derived parameters (baseline abundances, fitted mean-OD association, realistic library sizes) with `sd_log_overdispersion = 0` (no random variation in overdispersion across taxa). The same simulation case is used throughout, with different filtering applied for different figures.

## Figure 2: Filtered Data (0.1% abundance threshold)

This figure shows method performance on filtered simulated data (taxa with mean abundance <0.1% removed) with differential overdispersion but no differential abundance.

```{r case-4.5-load-params}
# Load BritoIL_2016 parameters for Figure 2 (Setting DO_no_sd)
# Load the sccomp results
result_case4_5 <- readRDS(sccomp_file_path)

# Extract fit object
fit_case4_5 <- attr(result_case4_5, "fit")

# Get basic dataset dimensions from the result
model_input_case4_5 <- attr(result_case4_5, "model_input")

# Extract N (samples) and M (taxa/cell groups) from model_input list
if (!is.null(model_input_case4_5) && is.list(model_input_case4_5)) {
  n_samples_real_case4_5 <- model_input_case4_5$N
  n_taxa_real_case4_5 <- model_input_case4_5$M
} else {
  # Fallback: use the cell_group column in result (unique taxa)
  n_taxa_real_case4_5 <- length(unique(result_case4_5$cell_group))
  n_samples_real_case4_5 <- 178
}

# Extract baseline abundance distribution (intercept effects for composition)
intercept_effects_case4_5 <- result_case4_5 %>%
  filter(parameter == "Intercept") %>%
  pull(c_effect)

# Extract mean-overdispersion association coefficient (k)
precision_params_case4_5 <- extract_precision_trend_params(fit_case4_5)
prec_slope_case4_5 <- precision_params_case4_5$prec_slope
k_realistic_case4_5 <- prec_slope_case4_5  # k = prec_slope for simulator

rm(result_case4_5)
gc()
```

```{r figure-2-setup}
# Figure 2 is about DO (differential overdispersion), not DA
# Row 1 uses delta DO = 0.5 from Setting DO_no_sd
# Load required targets
sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_jobs <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_jobs", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")

# Allow running this chunk standalone in IDE sessions where setup may be skipped.
if (!exists("simulate_case_realistic_taxa")) {
  targets_script_local <- file.path(getwd(), "_targets_BritoIL_2016.R")
  if (!file.exists(targets_script_local)) {
    qmd_dir_local <- dirname(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE))
    targets_script_local <- file.path(qmd_dir_local, "_targets_BritoIL_2016.R")
  }
  source(targets_script_local)
}

# Recreate the simulation for delta = 0.5 (matching the DO_no_sd sweep)
# This matches the simulation used in the DO_no_sd sweep
# 
# IMPORTANT: intercept_dispersion is the intercept 'c' in log(σ) = -k·log(μ) + c
# - Higher intercept_dispersion → Higher log(σ) → Higher σ → MORE overdispersion
# - Lower intercept_dispersion → Lower log(σ) → Lower σ → LESS overdispersion
# 
# For differential overdispersion (IBD > non-IBD):
# - non-IBD: intercept_dispersion = intercept_base (e.g., -6)
# - IBD: intercept_dispersion = intercept_base + delta (e.g., -5.5)
# - Since -5.5 > -6, IBD has HIGHER σ and MORE overdispersion ✓
#
# Note: This is NOT precision! Precision ≈ 1/σ, so higher σ = lower precision.
target_delta <- 0.5
intercept_base <- -6
intercept_disp_cohort <- c(intercept_base, intercept_base + target_delta)
intercept_disp_matrix <- outer(intercept_disp_cohort, rep(1, sim_params$n_taxa))
base_seed <- unique(sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_jobs$seed)[1]

source("_targets_BritoIL_2016.R")

sim_realistic_taxa <- simulate_case_realistic_taxa(
  slope_vector = rep(0, sim_params$n_taxa),  # No DA
  mu_inv_softmax = sim_params$mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic,
  log_dispersion_assoc = 0,
  intercept_dispersion_matrix = intercept_disp_matrix,
  sd_log_overdispersion = 0,
  seed = base_seed,
  n_taxa = sim_params$n_taxa,
  n_samples_per_group = sim_params$n_samples_per_group,
  library_size_mean = sim_params$library_size_mean,
  library_size_sd = sim_params$library_size_sd
) |> synthetic_sim_add_two_group_factor(c("stool", "oralcavity"))

# Filter and run analysis to get full results for plotting
# Use 0.1% threshold for Figure 2 (as stated in the text)
threshold_fig2 <- 0.001  # 0.1% threshold for Figure 2
sim_realistic_taxa_filtered <- sim_realistic_taxa
sim_realistic_taxa_filtered$count_long <- filter_by_abundance_cases(
  sim_realistic_taxa$count_long, 
  threshold = threshold_fig2
)

# Check that we have enough samples and taxa after filtering
n_samples_after <- length(unique(sim_realistic_taxa_filtered$count_long$sample_id))
n_taxa_after <- length(unique(sim_realistic_taxa_filtered$count_long$taxon_id))
samples_by_group <- sim_realistic_taxa_filtered$count_long %>%
  dplyr::distinct(sample_id, group) %>%
  dplyr::count(group)
cat("After filtering (threshold =", threshold_fig2, "):\n")
cat("  Samples:", n_samples_after, "\n")
cat("  Taxa:", n_taxa_after, "\n")
cat("  Samples by group:\n")
print(samples_by_group)

if (n_samples_after < 2) {
  stop("Not enough samples after filtering (need at least 2, got ", n_samples_after, ")")
}
if (n_taxa_after < 3) {
  stop("Not enough taxa after filtering (need at least 3, got ", n_taxa_after, ")")
}
if (nrow(samples_by_group) < 2 || any(samples_by_group$n < 1)) {
  stop("Not all groups have samples after filtering")
}

# Run analysis to get full results for plotting
analysis_realistic_taxa_filtered <- run_analysis_job(
  sim_r = sim_realistic_taxa_filtered,
  return_full = TRUE
)
```

```{r figure-2-filtered-comparison}
# sim_realistic_taxa_filtered is already created in figure-2-setup chunk
# Just verify it exists and get statistics

n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa <- length(unique(sim_realistic_taxa$count_long$taxon_id))
cat("=== Figure 2 Filtering Statistics ===\n")
cat("Number of taxa BEFORE filtering:", n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, "\n")
```
