```{r figure-2-filtered-comparison}
n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa <- length(unique(sim_realistic_taxa_filtered$count_long$taxon_id))
cat("Number of taxa AFTER 0.1% filtering:", n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa, "\n")
cat("Percentage retained:", round(100 * n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa / n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, 1), "%\n")
cat("=====================================\n\n")

# Store for later use
cat("\n**Figure 2 Filtering Summary:**\n")
cat("- Before filtering:", n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, "taxa\n")
cat("- After 0.1% filtering:", n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa, "taxa\n")
cat("- Percentage retained:", round(100 * n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa / n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, 1), "%\n")

# Display filtering statistics
cat("\n")
cat("**Figure 2 Filtering Statistics:**\n")
cat("- Number of taxa BEFORE filtering:", n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, "\n")
cat("- Number of taxa AFTER 0.1% filtering:", n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa, "\n")
cat("- Percentage retained:", round(100 * n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa / n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, 1), "%\n")
cat("\n")

# Format analysis results for plotting (already filtered in the simulation)
cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(
  analysis_results = analysis_realistic_taxa_filtered,
  case_label = "Setting DO_no_sd (filtered, delta = 0.5)"
)
```

```{r figure-2-DO-sweep-data}
# Load Setting DO_no_sd FPR data for FNR/centroid plots
sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_fpr <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_fpr", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
```


```{r fig-2-components}
# Figure 2 components (Setting DO_no_sd filtered)
cols_prgn <- c(
  stool = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[10],
  oralcavity = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[2]
)

p_std_realistic_taxa_filtered <- cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$stats %>% dplyr::filter(method == "permdisp_bray") %>% dplyr::pull(p_value)
se_std_realistic_taxa_filtered <- cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$stats %>% dplyr::filter(method == "permdisp_bray") %>% dplyr::pull(p_se)
n_std_realistic_taxa_filtered <- cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$stats %>% dplyr::filter(method == "permdisp_bray") %>% dplyr::pull(n_perm)

std_gg_realistic_taxa_filtered <- plot_betadisper_pcoa_and_boxplot(
  cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$bd$standard,
  "PERMDISP (Bray–Curtis)",
  cols = cols_prgn,
  p_value = p_std_realistic_taxa_filtered,
  p_se = se_std_realistic_taxa_filtered,
  n_perm = n_std_realistic_taxa_filtered,
  show_group_legend = TRUE,
  horizontal_box = TRUE
)
# Use delta = 2 simulation for alpha plot in PCoA grid
alpha_plot_realistic_taxa_filtered <- plot_alpha_diversity_scatter(sim_realistic_taxa_filtered, case_label = "Setting DO_no_sd (filtered)") +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = cols_prgn, guide = "none") +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = cols_prgn, guide = "none")

disp_scatter_realistic_taxa_filtered <- plot_mean_dispersion_scatter(sim_realistic_taxa_filtered, case_label = "Setting DO_no_sd (filtered)", cols = cols_prgn)

# Filter distances for non-IBD methods
distances_non_ibd_realistic_taxa_filtered <- cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$distances_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method_char = as.character(method)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!startsWith(method_char, "ibd")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method_label = sapply(method_char, pretty_method_label_box)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method_group = dplyr::case_when(
    method_label %in% c("Aitchison", "robust Aitchison") ~ "Aitchison methods",
    TRUE ~ "Other methods"
  ))

# Create boxplot panel for all methods (excluding IBD) with facets
p_boxplots_all_realistic_taxa_filtered <- ggplot(distances_non_ibd_realistic_taxa_filtered, aes(x = method_label, y = distance, fill = group)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outlier.size = 0.1, alpha = 0.85, linewidth = 0.25, outlier.stroke = 0, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  geom_jitter(size = 1.0, alpha = 0.25, stroke = 0, position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.8, jitter.width = 0.12)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_prgn, name = NULL) +
  facet_wrap(~ method_group, nrow = 1, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Distance to centroid") +
  theme_multipanel +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),
  )

# Create PCoA plots for all methods (excluding IBD) using delta = 2 simulation (original)
# Diagnostic: Verify the actual delta value in the simulation and centroid distances
# Extract parameters from simulation
intercept_dispersion_realistic_taxa <- unique(sim_realistic_taxa$cohort_log_sigma)
sd_log_overdispersion_realistic_taxa <- if("sd_log_overdispersion" %in% names(sim_realistic_taxa$parameters)) sim_realistic_taxa$parameters$sd_log_overdispersion else 0
slope_vector_realistic_taxa <- sim_realistic_taxa$slope_vector

cat("\n=== Figure 2 Row 1: Simulation Parameters ===\n")
cat("Simulation source: Setting DO_no_sd with delta = 0.5\n")
cat("Simulation parameters:\n")
cat("  intercept_dispersion (log) - non-IBD:", round(intercept_dispersion_realistic_taxa[1], 4), "\n")
cat("  intercept_dispersion (log) - IBD:", round(intercept_dispersion_realistic_taxa[2], 4), "\n")
delta_od <- intercept_dispersion_realistic_taxa[2] - intercept_dispersion_realistic_taxa[1]
cat("  Differential overdispersion (delta, log):", round(delta_od, 4), "\n")
cat("  Differential overdispersion (delta, linear scale, fold change):", round(exp(delta_od), 4), "x\n")
cat("  sd_log_overdispersion:", sd_log_overdispersion_realistic_taxa, "\n")
cat("  slope_vector (DA):", if(all(slope_vector_realistic_taxa == 0)) "all zeros (no DA)" else "has non-zero values", "\n")
cat("\n*** SUMMARY: Figure 2 Row 1 uses delta OD =", round(delta_od, 4), "on log (", round(exp(delta_od), 2), "x fold change) ***\n")
cat("*** Expected: delta OD = 0.5 (non-IBD: -6, IBD: -5.5) ***\n")

# Check all methods for centroid distances
cat("\nCentroid distances across all methods:\n")
for (method_key in names(cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$bd_by_method)) {
  if (startsWith(method_key, "ibd")) next
  bd_check <- cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$bd_by_method[[method_key]]
  if (is.null(bd_check)) next
  
  group_dists <- bd_check$group.distances
  centroids <- bd_check$centroids
  
  method_label <- pretty_method_label_box(method_key)
  cat("\n", method_label, ":\n", sep = "")
  cat("  non-IBD mean distance to centroid:", round(group_dists[1], 6), "\n")
  cat("  IBD mean distance to centroid:", round(group_dists[2], 6), "\n")
  cat("  Difference (dispersion delta):", round(group_dists[2] - group_dists[1], 6), "\n")
  
  if (nrow(centroids) == 2 && ncol(centroids) >= 2) {
    # Distance in first 2 PCoA dimensions (what's shown in the plot)
    centroid_dist_pcoa12 <- sqrt(sum((centroids[1, 1:2] - centroids[2, 1:2])^2))
    hull_scale <- compute_centroid_distance_pct_hull_radius(bd_check)
    # Distance in all dimensions
    centroid_dist_all <- sqrt(sum((centroids[1,] - centroids[2,])^2))
    
    # Get eigenvalues to see variance explained
    eig <- bd_check$eig
    eig_pct <- if (!is.null(eig) && length(eig) > 0) {
      round(100 * eig[1:min(5, length(eig))] / sum(eig[eig > 0]), 2)
    } else NULL
    
    cat("  Centroid positions (PCoA 1-2):\n")
    cat("    non-IBD:", paste(round(centroids[1, 1:2], 6), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    cat("    IBD:", paste(round(centroids[2, 1:2], 6), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    cat("  Distance between centroids (PCoA 1-2):", round(centroid_dist_pcoa12, 8), "\n")
    cat(
      "  Centroid distance as % of mean hull radius:",
      round(hull_scale$pct_of_mean_hull_radius, 2), "%\n"
    )
    cat("  Distance between centroids (all dims):", round(centroid_dist_all, 8), "\n")
    if (!is.null(eig_pct)) {
      cat("  Variance explained (first 5 dims):", paste(paste0(round(eig_pct, 1), "%"), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    }
    
    # Check if distance is in higher dimensions
    if (ncol(centroids) > 2) {
      dist_by_dim <- sapply(1:min(10, ncol(centroids)), function(i) {
        abs(centroids[1, i] - centroids[2, i])
      })
      cat("  Centroid separation by dimension (first 10):", paste(round(dist_by_dim, 6), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    }
  }
} 
cat("\n=== End diagnostic ===\n\n")

non_ibd_methods_realistic_taxa_filtered <- names(cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$bd_by_method)[!startsWith(names(cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$bd_by_method), "ibd")]

pcoa_plots_all_realistic_taxa_filtered <- purrr::imap(non_ibd_methods_realistic_taxa_filtered, function(method_key, idx) {
  bd <- cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$bd_by_method[[method_key]]
  
  method_label <- pretty_method_label_box(method_key)
  p_val <- cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered$stats %>% 
    dplyr::filter(method == method_key) %>% 
    dplyr::pull(p_value)
  
  gg <- plot_betadisper_pcoa_and_boxplot(
    bd,
    title_prefix = method_label,
    cols = cols_prgn,
    p_value = p_val,
    show_group_legend = FALSE,
    horizontal_box = FALSE
  )
  
  gg$pcoa <- gg$pcoa + 
    ggtitle(method_label) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 7, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 2))
    )
  
  if (idx > 1) {
    gg$pcoa <- gg$pcoa + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
  }
  gg$pcoa
})

# Create PCoA grid with 5 PCoA plots + 1 spacer (2x3 grid)
if (length(pcoa_plots_all_realistic_taxa_filtered) > 0) {
  # Combine PCoA plots with spacer
  all_plots_realistic_taxa_filtered <- c(pcoa_plots_all_realistic_taxa_filtered, list(plot_spacer()))
  p_pcoa_all_realistic_taxa_filtered <- patchwork::wrap_plots(all_plots_realistic_taxa_filtered, nrow = 2, ncol = 3) + patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")
} else {
  p_pcoa_all_realistic_taxa_filtered <- ggplot() + theme_void()
}

# Calculate False Negative Rate (FNR) for each delta, method, and threshold
# Note: sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_fpr is already loaded in figure-2-DO-sweep-data chunk
fnr_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered <- sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_fpr %>%
  dplyr::filter(!grepl("^IBD", method, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(delta, method, threshold) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_sims = dplyr::n(),
    n_fn = sum(p_value > 0.05),
    FNR = n_fn / n_sims,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    method = coerce_method_levels(method),
    threshold_label = dplyr::case_when(
      threshold == 0.0 ~ "unfiltered",
      threshold == 0.005 ~ "0.5%",
      threshold == 0.001 ~ "0.1%",
      TRUE ~ as.character(threshold)
    ),
    alpha_val = dplyr::case_when(
      threshold == 0.005 ~ 1.0,
      threshold == 0.001 ~ 0.55,
      threshold == 0.0 ~ 0.25,
      TRUE ~ 1.0
    )
  )

p_sweep_fnr_realistic_taxa_filtered <- ggplot(fnr_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered, aes(x = delta, y = FNR, color = method, alpha = alpha_val)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.1, aes(group = interaction(method, threshold))) +
  geom_point(size = 1.0, stroke = 0, aes(group = interaction(method, threshold))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = roc_colors_all, breaks = method_levels, drop = FALSE, name = NULL) +
  scale_alpha_identity(guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = {
    breaks <- unique(fnr_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered$delta)
    breaks[breaks == 0 | breaks >= 0.25]
  }, labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Differential dispersion (log)",
    y = "False negative rate"
  ) +
  theme_multipanel

# Calculate centroid separation as % of average hull radius for each delta, method, and threshold
centroid_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered_raw <- sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_fpr %>%
  dplyr::filter(!grepl("^IBD", method, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(delta, method, threshold) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_sims = dplyr::n(),
    mean_centroid_pct = mean(centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_centroid_pct = sd(centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    method = coerce_method_levels(method),
    threshold_label = dplyr::case_when(
      threshold == 0.0 ~ "unfiltered",
      threshold == 0.005 ~ "0.5%",
      threshold == 0.001 ~ "0.1%",
      TRUE ~ as.character(threshold)
    ),
    alpha_val = dplyr::case_when(
      threshold == 0.005 ~ 1.0,
      threshold == 0.001 ~ 0.55,
      threshold == 0.0 ~ 0.25,
      TRUE ~ 1.0
    )
  )

centroid_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered <- centroid_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered_raw

# Mixed-effects association model:
# centroid shift (%) ~ differential dispersion (delta)
# with random intercept and slope per method-threshold combination.
lmm_data_fig2 <- sweep_setting_DO_no_sd_fpr %>%
  dplyr::filter(!grepl("^IBD", method, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    method_threshold = interaction(method, threshold, drop = TRUE)
  )

lmm_full_fig2 <- lme4::lmer(
  centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius ~ delta + (1 + delta | method_threshold),
  data = lmm_data_fig2,
  REML = FALSE
)
lmm_null_fig2 <- lme4::lmer(
  centroid_sep_pct_hull_radius ~ 1 + (1 + delta | method_threshold),
  data = lmm_data_fig2,
  REML = FALSE
)

lmm_lrt_fig2 <- stats::anova(lmm_null_fig2, lmm_full_fig2)
lmm_p_delta_fig2 <- lmm_lrt_fig2$`Pr(>Chisq)`[2]
lmm_fixef_fig2 <- lme4::fixef(lmm_full_fig2)
lmm_intercept_fig2 <- unname(lmm_fixef_fig2["(Intercept)"])
lmm_slope_fig2 <- unname(lmm_fixef_fig2["delta"])

fmt_p_lmm <- function(p) {
  if (!is.finite(p)) return("NA")
  if (p < 0.001) return("<0.001")
  sprintf("%.3f", p)
}
lmm_label_fig2 <- paste0("p = ", fmt_p_lmm(lmm_p_delta_fig2))

p_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered <- ggplot(centroid_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered, aes(x = delta, y = mean_centroid_pct, color = method, alpha = alpha_val)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_centroid_pct - se_centroid_pct, 
                  ymax = mean_centroid_pct + se_centroid_pct, fill = method, group = interaction(method, threshold)), 
              alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.1, aes(group = interaction(method, threshold))) +
  geom_point(size = 1.0, stroke = 0, aes(group = interaction(method, threshold))) +
  geom_abline(
    intercept = lmm_intercept_fig2,
    slope = lmm_slope_fig2,
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.1,
    linetype = "dotted"
  ) +
  annotate(
    geom = "text",
    x = 0.1,
    y = 18,
    label = lmm_label_fig2,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = 1,
    size = 2.6,
    fontface = "plain",
    color = "black"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = roc_colors_all, breaks = method_levels, drop = FALSE, name = NULL) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = roc_colors_all, breaks = method_levels, drop = FALSE, guide = "none") +
  scale_alpha_identity(guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = {
    breaks <- unique(centroid_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered$delta)
    breaks[breaks == 0 | breaks >= 0.25]
  }, labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels, limits = c(0, NA)) +
  labs(
    x = "Differential dispersion (log)",
    y = "Centroid shift (% of mean hull radius)"
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  theme_multipanel +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 0.1, color = "grey90"),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

# Get top 30 most abundant taxa for whisker plot (Figure 2, Setting DO_no_sd filtered)
# Use the ORIGINAL sim_realistic_taxa (before filtering) to identify top 30 taxa, then filter those
# This ensures we get the top 30 from the full dataset, not just what remains after 0.1% filtering
taxon_abundance_realistic_taxa_original <- sim_realistic_taxa$count_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / library_size) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(taxon_id) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean_prop = mean(proportion, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(mean_prop)) %>%
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 30) %>%
  dplyr::pull(taxon_id)

# Check how many of the top 30 remain after filtering
top30_after_filter <- sum(taxon_abundance_realistic_taxa_original %in% unique(sim_realistic_taxa_filtered$count_long$taxon_id))

cat("\n**Whisker Plot Taxa Selection:**\n")
cat("- Top 30 taxa selected from original", n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, "taxa\n")
cat("- Top 30 taxa remaining after 0.1% filter:", top30_after_filter, "out of 30\n")
cat("- Taxa shown in whisker plot:", top30_after_filter, "\n")
cat("\n")

# Create filtered simulation with only top 30 taxa for whisker plot
# Use the top 30 from original, but show data from filtered simulation
sim_realistic_taxa_filtered_top30 <- sim_realistic_taxa_filtered
sim_realistic_taxa_filtered_top30$count_long <- sim_realistic_taxa_filtered$count_long %>%
  dplyr::filter(taxon_id %in% taxon_abundance_realistic_taxa_original)

# Display filtering statistics
cat("\n**Case 4.5 Filtering Statistics:**\n")
cat("- Number of taxa BEFORE filtering:", n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, "\n")
cat("- Number of taxa AFTER 0.1% filtering:", n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa, "\n")
cat("- Percentage retained:", round(100 * n_taxa_after_filter_realistic_taxa / n_taxa_before_filter_realistic_taxa, 1), "%\n")
cat("- Top 30 taxa remaining after filter:", top30_after_filter, "out of 30\n")
cat("- Taxa shown in whisker plot:", top30_after_filter, "\n")
cat("\n")

# Create whisker plot for top 20 most abundant filtered taxa
arcsin_sqrt_trans_realistic_taxa <- scales::trans_new(
  name = "arcsin_sqrt",
  transform = function(x) asin(sqrt(pmax(0, pmin(1, x)))),
  inverse = function(y) sin(y)^2,
  breaks = scales::extended_breaks(),
  domain = c(0, 1)
)

d_whisker_realistic_taxa_all <- sim_realistic_taxa_filtered$count_long %>%
  dplyr::filter(taxon_id %in% taxon_abundance_realistic_taxa_original) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / library_size) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(group, taxon_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
    mean_prop = mean(proportion, na.rm = TRUE),
    p_low = stats::quantile(proportion, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    p_high = stats::quantile(proportion, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
  )

taxon_order_whisker_realistic_taxa_all <- d_whisker_realistic_taxa_all %>%
  dplyr::group_by(taxon_id) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(overall_mean = mean(mean_prop, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(overall_mean)) %>%
  dplyr::pull(taxon_id)

d_whisker_realistic_taxa_all <- d_whisker_realistic_taxa_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(taxon_id = factor(taxon_id, levels = taxon_order_whisker_realistic_taxa_all))

prop_breaks_whisker_realistic_taxa_all <- c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.5)
prop_breaks_whisker_realistic_taxa_all <- prop_breaks_whisker_realistic_taxa_all[prop_breaks_whisker_realistic_taxa_all <= max(d_whisker_realistic_taxa_all$p_high, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1]

p_whisker_realistic_taxa_filtered_all <- ggplot(d_whisker_realistic_taxa_all, aes(x = taxon_id, y = mean_prop, ymin = p_low, ymax = p_high, color = group)) +
  ggplot2::geom_linerange(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.6), linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 1.0, stroke = 0) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = cols_prgn, name = NULL) +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
    trans = arcsin_sqrt_trans_realistic_taxa,
    breaks = prop_breaks_whisker_realistic_taxa_all,
    labels = function(x) gsub("\\.0%", "%", scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.1)(x))
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    x = "Taxon (ordered by mean proportion)",
    y = "Proportion (arcsin-sqrt scale, 95% CI)"
  ) +
  theme_multipanel +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

# Second row: PCoA grid on left, sweep plots on right (whisker plot moved to first row)
fig_2_case4_5 <- (p_pcoa_all_realistic_taxa_filtered | (p_sweep_fnr_realistic_taxa_filtered / p_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered) + patchwork::plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))) + 
  patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect", widths = c(1, 1)) &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"), legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'), legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r fig-2-combined-filtered, fig.width=16, fig.height=14}
#| fig-cap: "Setting DO_no_sd (filtered): Differential overdispersion with no differential abundance, with abundance filtering. Realistic number of taxa, no random variation in overdispersion (0.1% threshold)."

# First row: whisker plot (all filtered taxa) | alpha diversity | boxplot
fig_2_row1_new <- (p_whisker_realistic_taxa_filtered_all | alpha_plot_realistic_taxa_filtered | p_boxplots_all_realistic_taxa_filtered) + 
  patchwork::plot_layout(widths = c(0.35, 0.325, 0.325), guides = "collect") &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"), legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'), legend.position = "bottom")

# Combine rows: Row 1 (spacer | whisker | alpha), Row 2 (sweep plots | PCoA grid)
fig_2 <- (fig_2_row1_new / fig_2_case4_5) + 
  patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect", heights = c(1, 2)) &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"), legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'), legend.position = "bottom")

fig_2
```

```{r fig-2-save-pdf}
#| echo: false
ggsave("Figure_2_BritoIL_2016.pdf", fig_2, width = 183, height = 138, units = "mm")
```

```{r cleanup-after-figure-2}
# Release heavy Figure 2 objects before next sections
rm(list = intersect(
  c(
    "sim_realistic_taxa", "sim_realistic_taxa_filtered", "analysis_realistic_taxa_filtered",
    "cmp_realistic_taxa_filtered", "std_gg_realistic_taxa_filtered",
    "p_pcoa_all_realistic_taxa_filtered", "p_boxplots_all_realistic_taxa_filtered",
    "alpha_plot_realistic_taxa_filtered", "disp_scatter_realistic_taxa_filtered",
    "p_whisker_realistic_taxa_filtered_all", "fig_2_case4_5", "fig_2_row1_new", "fig_2",
    "distances_non_ibd_realistic_taxa_filtered", "d_whisker_realistic_taxa_all",
    "taxon_abundance_realistic_taxa_original", "sim_realistic_taxa_filtered_top30",
    "centroid_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered", "centroid_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered_raw",
    "p_sweep_fnr_realistic_taxa_filtered", "p_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered"
  ),
  ls()
))
invisible(gc())
```

# Simulation no-DO (No Differential Overdispersion)

This section tests method performance when there is **differential abundance but NO differential overdispersion**. This evaluates whether methods produce false positives from composition differences when there is no true differential dispersion.

## Figure 3: Setting DA_no_sd (filtered) and Setting DA_sd (filtered)

This figure shows method performance on filtered simulated data (taxa with mean abundance <0.1% removed) with differential abundance but no differential overdispersion.

```{r shared-setting-params}
# Parameters for Setting DA_no_sd
intercept_disp_DA_no_sd <- intercept_disp_realistic
sigma_DA_no_sd <- sigma_realistic
mu_base_DA_no_sd <- mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic
k_fixed_DA_no_sd <- 0

# Parameters for Setting DA_sd
intercept_disp_DA_sd <- intercept_disp_realistic
sigma_DA_sd <- sigma_realistic
mu_base_DA_sd <- mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic
k_fixed_DA_sd <- 0

standard_distance_methods_setting5 <- c("bray", "aitchison", "robust.aitchison", "jaccard", "hellinger")
group_levels <- c("stool", "oralcavity")

cols_prgn_setting5 <- c(
  stool = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[10],
  oralcavity = RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(11, "PRGn")[2]
)

# Whole-taxon (column) filter only: mean proportion is computed per taxon across *all* samples,
# then taxa below the threshold are removed entirely. No per-cell or per-sample-row dropping.
filter_by_abundance_for_plot <- function(count_long, min_abundance) {
  taxon_means <- count_long %>%
    dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / library_size) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(taxon_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(mean_prop = mean(proportion, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  keep_taxa <- taxon_means %>% dplyr::filter(mean_prop >= min_abundance) %>% dplyr::pull(taxon_id)
  count_long %>% dplyr::filter(taxon_id %in% keep_taxa)
}
```

### Setting DA_no_sd (filtered): Differential abundance, no sd on overdispersion, with abundance filtering (<0.1%)

This setting uses the same simulated data as Setting DA_no_sd but filters out taxa with mean abundance <0.1% before computing distances and running PERMDISP. This tests whether the trends observed in Setting DA_no_sd are driven by low abundance taxa.

**Note:** A 0.1% mean abundance threshold is more appropriate than 5% for microbiome data, as 5% would remove most taxa. PERMDISP requires at least 3 taxa to function, but more taxa are preferable for stable results.

```{r figure-3-seed-selection}
# Seed selection for Figure 3 (PCoA plots and top row)
# Change seed_idx to try different seeds and find a more representative example
seed_idx_fig3 <- 1
```

```{r setting-DA-no-sd-filtered-pcoa-plots}
# Create PCoA panel for Setting DA_no_sd (filtered) using sweep analysis outputs
# Use threshold = 0.001 (0.1%) for filtered plots
sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
# Use the same seed for all cases (1, 10, 40 DA) for consistency
available_seeds <- unique(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs$seed)
seed_fig3_pcoa <- available_seeds[min(seed_idx_fig3, length(available_seeds))]
threshold_filtered <- 0.001  # Use 0.1% threshold for filtered plots

# Get analysis results for filtered (0.5% threshold) - all using the same seed
analysis_1_raw <- get_sweep_analysis_with_threshold(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res, sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold, seed_fig3_pcoa, 1, threshold_filtered)
analysis_10_raw <- get_sweep_analysis_with_threshold(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res, sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold, seed_fig3_pcoa, 10, threshold_filtered)
analysis_40_raw <- get_sweep_analysis_with_threshold(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res, sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold, seed_fig3_pcoa, 40, threshold_filtered)
if (is.null(analysis_1_raw)) stop("analysis_1_raw is NULL")
if (is.null(analysis_10_raw)) stop("analysis_10_raw is NULL")
if (is.null(analysis_40_raw)) stop("analysis_40_raw is NULL")

# Format analysis results for plotting
analysis_1 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(analysis_1_raw, case_label = "1 DA")
analysis_10 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(analysis_10_raw, case_label = "10 DA")
analysis_40 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(analysis_40_raw, case_label = "40 DA")

pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered <- plot_pcoa_grid_multiple_methods(
  analysis_list = list(analysis_1, analysis_10, analysis_40),
  n_da_labels = c("1", "10", "40")
)

# Verify the plot was created
if (is.null(pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered)) {
  stop("pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered is NULL after creation")
}

# Free temporary analysis objects; keep shared sweep targets for downstream chunks
rm(list = intersect(
  c(
    "available_seeds", "seed_fig3_pcoa", "threshold_filtered",
    "analysis_1_raw", "analysis_10_raw", "analysis_40_raw",
    "analysis_1", "analysis_10", "analysis_40"
  ),
  ls()
))
invisible(gc())
```

```{r setting-DA-no-sd-filtered-top-panel}
# Get FPR data for all thresholds (0.005 opaque, 0.001 lighter, 0.0 lightest)
fpr_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_fpr", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")

p_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered_fpr <- plot_da_taxa_sweep_results(
  fpr_data = fpr_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered,
  setting_name = paste0("Setting DA_no_sd (filtered): Differential abundance, no sd on overdispersion, with abundance filter (≥0.1%) (n=", n_samples, ", p=", n_taxa, 
                       ", c=", round(intercept_disp_DA_no_sd, 2), 
                       ", σ=", round(sigma_DA_no_sd, 5), 
                       ", k=", round(k_fixed_DA_no_sd, 3), ")"),
  sigma_val = sigma_DA_no_sd,
  intercept_disp_val = intercept_disp_DA_no_sd,
  x_limits = c(0, 40),
  x_breaks = c(0, 10, 20, 30, 40)
)

```


### PERMANOVA on Setting DA_no_sd (filtered): TPR with true differential composition

This panel uses the same filtered simulations as the first Figure 3 sweep (**Setting DA_no_sd**, `k = 0`, `sd_log_overdispersion = 0`) and evaluates **PERMANOVA true positive rate** (detection rate) when true differential abundance is present (`n_da_taxa > 0`).

```{r setting-DA-no-sd-permanova-tpr, fig.width=12, fig.height=8}
permanova_da_tpr <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_tpr", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    method_label = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$method == "permanova_bray" ~ "Bray–Curtis",
      .data$method == "permanova_aitchison" ~ "Aitchison",
      .data$method == "permanova_robust.aitchison" ~ "robust Aitchison",
      .data$method == "permanova_robust_aitchison" ~ "robust Aitchison",
      .data$method == "permanova_jaccard" ~ "Jaccard",
      .data$method == "permanova_hellinger" ~ "Hellinger",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    alpha_val = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$threshold == 0.0 ~ 0.25,
      .data$threshold == 0.005 ~ 1.0,
      .data$threshold == 0.001 ~ 0.55,
      TRUE ~ 1.0
    ),
    threshold_label = dplyr::case_when(
      .data$threshold == 0.0 ~ "unfiltered",
      .data$threshold == 0.005 ~ "0.5%",
      .data$threshold == 0.001 ~ "0.1%",
      TRUE ~ as.character(.data$threshold)
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data$method_label)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method_label = coerce_method_levels(.data$method_label))

p_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_tpr <- ggplot(
  permanova_da_tpr,
  aes(x = n_da_taxa, y = TPR, color = method_label, alpha = alpha_val)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.1) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.1, aes(group = interaction(method_label, threshold))) +
  geom_point(size = 1.0, stroke = 0, aes(group = interaction(method_label, threshold))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = roc_colors_all, breaks = method_levels, drop = FALSE, name = NULL) +
  scale_alpha_identity(guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 10, 20, 30, 40),
    limits = c(0, 40),
    labels = format_axis_labels
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = format_axis_labels, limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of DA taxa",
    y = "True positive rate (PERMANOVA)"
  ) +
  theme_multipanel

# Keep only the final ggplot object
rm(list = intersect(c("permanova_da_tpr"), ls()))
invisible(gc())
```

```{r figure-3-scatter}
standard_methods_for_scatter <- c("Bray–Curtis", "Aitchison", "robust Aitchison", "Jaccard", "Hellinger")

# Figure 2 provides the *power* side for differential dispersion.
# Summarize **FNR** over delta > 0 (exclude null), by method and threshold.
fnr_fig2 <- fnr_sweep_realistic_taxa_filtered %>%
  dplyr::filter(delta > 0) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(method, threshold) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    avg_fnr = mean(FNR, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(method %in% standard_methods_for_scatter) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    threshold_label = dplyr::case_when(
      threshold == 0.005 ~ "0.5%",
      threshold == 0.001 ~ "0.1%",
      threshold == 0.0 ~ "unfiltered",
      TRUE ~ as.character(threshold)
    ),
    alpha_val = dplyr::case_when(
      threshold == 0.005 ~ 1.0,
      threshold == 0.001 ~ 0.55,
      threshold == 0.0 ~ 0.25,
      TRUE ~ 1.0
    )
  )

cat("\n=== Average FNR from Figure 2 (delta > 0) ===\n")
print(fnr_fig2 %>% dplyr::arrange(method))

# Figure 3 provides the *FPR* side from the DA sweep:
# Average FPR across all n_da_taxa values for Setting DA_no_sd, by method and threshold.
fpr_fig3_data <- fpr_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered %>%
    dplyr::mutate(method_label = dplyr::case_when(
      method == "permdisp_bray" ~ "Bray–Curtis",
      method == "permdisp_aitchison" ~ "Aitchison",
      method == "permdisp_robust.aitchison" ~ "robust Aitchison",
    method == "permdisp_robust_aitchison" ~ "robust Aitchison",
      method == "permdisp_jaccard" ~ "Jaccard",
      method == "permdisp_hellinger" ~ "Hellinger",
    as.character(method) %in% standard_methods_for_scatter ~ as.character(method),
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(method_label))

fpr_col <- if ("FPR" %in% names(fpr_fig3_data)) "FPR" else "fpr"

# Average FPR across all n_da_taxa values for each method and threshold
fpr_fig3 <- fpr_fig3_data %>%
  dplyr::group_by(method_label, threshold) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    avg_fpr = mean(.data[[fpr_col]], na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rename(method = method_label) %>%
  dplyr::filter(method %in% standard_methods_for_scatter) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    threshold_label = dplyr::case_when(
      threshold == 0.005 ~ "0.5%",
      threshold == 0.001 ~ "0.1%",
      threshold == 0.0 ~ "unfiltered",
      TRUE ~ as.character(threshold)
    ),
    alpha_val = dplyr::case_when(
      threshold == 0.005 ~ 1.0,
      threshold == 0.001 ~ 0.55,
      threshold == 0.0 ~ 0.25,
      TRUE ~ 1.0
    )
  )

cat("\n=== Average FPR from Figure 3 Setting DA_no_sd (filtered) (averaged across all n_da_taxa) ===\n")
print(fpr_fig3 %>% dplyr::arrange(method))

scatter_data <- fnr_fig2 %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(fpr_fig3, by = c("method", "threshold", "threshold_label", "alpha_val")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    method = factor(method, levels = standard_methods_for_scatter),
    threshold_label = factor(threshold_label, levels = c("0.5%", "0.1%", "unfiltered"))
  )

plot_data <- scatter_data %>% dplyr::filter(!is.na(avg_fnr) & !is.na(avg_fpr))

# Calculate linear regression manually for full-range line with SD bands
lm_fit <- lm(avg_fpr ~ avg_fnr, data = plot_data)
residual_sd <- sd(residuals(lm_fit))

# Create prediction data across full plot range (0 to max x)
x_range <- c(0, max(plot_data$avg_fnr, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1)  # Extend slightly beyond data
x_pred <- seq(x_range[1], x_range[2], length.out = 200)
pred_data <- data.frame(avg_fnr = x_pred)
pred_data$avg_fpr <- predict(lm_fit, newdata = pred_data)
pred_data$ymin <- pred_data$avg_fpr - residual_sd
pred_data$ymax <- pred_data$avg_fpr + residual_sd

p_scatter_fnr_fpr <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = avg_fnr, y = avg_fpr, color = method)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_ribbon(data = pred_data, aes(x = avg_fnr, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax), 
              inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = "lightgrey", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_line(data = pred_data, aes(x = avg_fnr, y = avg_fpr), 
            inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "grey", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = threshold_label, alpha = alpha_val), size = 1.1) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = dplyr::filter(plot_data, threshold == 0.005),
    aes(label = method),
    size = 3,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = roc_colors_all[standard_methods_for_scatter], breaks = standard_methods_for_scatter, drop = FALSE, name = NULL) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("0.5%" = 16, "0.1%" = 17, "unfiltered" = 15), name = "Threshold") +
  scale_alpha_identity(guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Average false negative rate (Figure 2; delta > 0)", limits = c(0, NA), labels = format_axis_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Average false positive rate (Figure 3 Setting DA_no_sd filtered)", limits = c(0, NA), labels = format_axis_labels) +
  theme_multipanel +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r figure-3-scatter-diagnostics}
# Print diagnostic information about FPR and FNR values used in performance plot
cat("\n=== Average FNR from Figure 2 (delta > 0) ===\n")
print(fnr_fig2 %>% dplyr::arrange(method))

cat("\n=== Average FPR from Figure 3 Setting DA_no_sd (filtered) (averaged across all n_da_taxa) ===\n")
print(fpr_fig3 %>% dplyr::arrange(method))
```

```{r figure-3-top-row}
# Create plots for 40 DA case (Setting DA_no_sd) for top row of Figure 3
# Use 40 DA simulation + analysis from sweep outputs (then filter for plotting)
if (!exists("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs", inherits = FALSE)) {
  sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
}
sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
if (!exists("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold", inherits = FALSE)) {
  sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
}
if (!exists("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res", inherits = FALSE)) {
  sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
}
# Use the same seed as PCoA plots for consistency (uses seed_idx_fig3 from figure-3-seed-selection chunk)
available_seeds_fig3 <- unique(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs$seed)
seed_40_da_fig3 <- available_seeds_fig3[min(seed_idx_fig3, length(available_seeds_fig3))]
# Use threshold = 0.001 (0.1%) for Figure 3
threshold_fig3 <- 0.001
idx_40_da <- get_sweep_index(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs, seed_40_da_fig3, 40)
sim_40_da <- sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim[[idx_40_da]]
sim_40_da_filtered <- sim_40_da
sim_40_da_filtered$count_long <- filter_by_abundance_for_plot(
  sim_40_da$count_long,
  0.001  # Use 0.1% threshold for Figure 3
)

# Run comparison for 40 DA case (filtered analysis from sweeps)
cmp_40_da <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(
  analysis_results = get_sweep_analysis_with_threshold(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res, sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold, seed_40_da_fig3, 40, threshold_fig3),
  case_label = "Setting DA_no_sd (40 DA, filtered)"
)

# Release large sweep containers once the single 40-DA slice is materialized
rm(list = intersect(
  c(
    "sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs", "sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_sim",
    "sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs_with_threshold", "sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res",
    "available_seeds_fig3", "idx_40_da"
  ),
  ls()
))
invisible(gc())

# Get top 30 most abundant taxa for whisker plot (from original unfiltered simulation)
taxon_abundance_40_da_original <- sim_40_da$count_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / library_size) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(taxon_id) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(mean_prop = mean(proportion, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(mean_prop)) %>%
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 30) %>%
  dplyr::pull(taxon_id)

# Check how many of the top 30 remain after filtering
top30_after_filter_40_da <- sum(taxon_abundance_40_da_original %in% unique(sim_40_da_filtered$count_long$taxon_id))

cat("\n**Figure 3 Whisker Plot Taxa Selection:**\n")
cat("- Top 30 taxa selected from original simulation\n")
cat("- Top 30 taxa remaining after 0.1% filter:", top30_after_filter_40_da, "out of 30\n")
cat("- Taxa shown in whisker plot:", top30_after_filter_40_da, "\n")
cat("\n")

# Create whisker plot for top 30 most abundant taxa (40 DA case, filtered)
arcsin_sqrt_trans_40_da <- scales::trans_new(
  name = "arcsin_sqrt",
  transform = function(x) asin(sqrt(pmax(0, pmin(1, x)))),
  inverse = function(y) sin(y)^2,
  breaks = scales::extended_breaks(),
  domain = c(0, 1)
)

d_whisker_40_da <- sim_40_da_filtered$count_long %>%
    dplyr::filter(taxon_id %in% taxon_abundance_40_da_original) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(proportion = count / library_size) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(group, taxon_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      mean_prop = mean(proportion, na.rm = TRUE),
      med_prop = stats::median(proportion, na.rm = TRUE),
      p_low = stats::quantile(proportion, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
      p_high = stats::quantile(proportion, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
# Whiskers are 2.5%–97.5% empirical quantiles. The point uses the **median**, not the mean:
# with zero-inflated proportions (many zeros + rare large counts), the **mean can lie above
# p_high** even though p_high is a valid upper quantile — then a mean dot looks like the
# "upper quantile is below the dot". The median always lies between p_low and p_high.
# With discrete counts, p_low == p_high often → zero-height interval (looks like a missing whisker).
n_w_zero_height <- sum(dplyr::coalesce(d_whisker_40_da$p_high - d_whisker_40_da$p_low, 0) <= 0, na.rm = TRUE)
cat(
  "- Whisker segments with zero height (p_low == p_high): ",
  n_w_zero_height, " of ", nrow(d_whisker_40_da), " group×taxon cells\n",
  sep = ""
)
  
taxon_order_whisker_40_da <- d_whisker_40_da %>%
    dplyr::group_by(taxon_id) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(overall_mean = mean(mean_prop, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(overall_mean)) %>%
    dplyr::pull(taxon_id)
  
d_whisker_40_da <- d_whisker_40_da %>%
  dplyr::mutate(taxon_id = factor(taxon_id, levels = taxon_order_whisker_40_da))
  
prop_breaks_whisker_40_da <- c(0.001, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.5)
prop_breaks_whisker_40_da <- prop_breaks_whisker_40_da[prop_breaks_whisker_40_da <= max(d_whisker_40_da$p_high, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.1]
  
p_whisker_40_da <- ggplot(d_whisker_40_da, aes(x = taxon_id, y = mean_prop, ymin = p_low, ymax = p_high, color = group)) +
    ggplot2::geom_linerange(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.6), linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.8) +
    ggplot2::geom_point(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 1.0, stroke = 0) +
  ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = cols_prgn_setting5, name = NULL) +
    ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
    trans = arcsin_sqrt_trans_40_da,
    breaks = prop_breaks_whisker_40_da,
      labels = function(x) gsub("\\.0%", "%", scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.1)(x))
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      x = "Taxon (ordered by mean proportion)",
      y = "Proportion (arcsin-sqrt scale, 95% CI)"
    ) +
    theme_multipanel +
    ggplot2::theme(
      axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.x = ggplot2::element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
# Create alpha diversity plot for 40 DA case (filtered)
alpha_plot_40_da <- plot_alpha_diversity_scatter(sim_40_da_filtered, case_label = "Setting DA_no_sd (40 DA, filtered)")
if (!is.null(alpha_plot_40_da)) {
  alpha_plot_40_da <- alpha_plot_40_da +
    ggplot2::scale_color_manual(values = cols_prgn_setting5, guide = "none") +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = cols_prgn_setting5, guide = "none")
} else {
  warning("alpha_plot_40_da is NULL")
}

# Create boxplots for 40 DA case (5 methods) with facets
distances_non_ibd_40_da <- cmp_40_da$distances_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method_char = as.character(method)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!startsWith(method_char, "ibd")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method_label = sapply(method_char, pretty_method_label_box)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(method_group = dplyr::case_when(
    method_label %in% c("Aitchison", "robust Aitchison") ~ "Aitchison methods",
    TRUE ~ "Other methods"
  ))

p_boxplots_40_da <- ggplot(distances_non_ibd_40_da, aes(x = method_label, y = distance, fill = group)) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.5, outlier.size = 0.1, alpha = 0.85, linewidth = 0.25, outlier.stroke = 0, position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  geom_jitter(size = 1.0, alpha = 0.25, stroke = 0, position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.8, jitter.width = 0.12)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_prgn_setting5, name = NULL) +
  facet_wrap(~ method_group, nrow = 1, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Distance to centroid") +
  theme_multipanel +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 6),
  )

# Create top row without spacer
p_figure3_row1 <- (p_whisker_40_da | alpha_plot_40_da | p_boxplots_40_da) + 
  patchwork::plot_layout(widths = c(0.35, 0.325, 0.325), guides = "collect") &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"), legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'))

# Drop heavy intermediates after composing the final row object
rm(list = intersect(
  c(
    "sim_40_da", "sim_40_da_filtered", "cmp_40_da", "seed_40_da_fig3", "threshold_fig3",
    "taxon_abundance_40_da_original", "top30_after_filter_40_da",
    "arcsin_sqrt_trans_40_da", "d_whisker_40_da", "taxon_order_whisker_40_da",
    "prop_breaks_whisker_40_da", "distances_non_ibd_40_da",
    "p_whisker_40_da", "alpha_plot_40_da", "p_boxplots_40_da"
  ),
  ls()
))
invisible(gc())
```

```{r figure-3, fig.width=16, fig.height=12}
#| fig-cap: "Figure 3. Top row: composition-level diagnostics for Setting DA_no_sd at 40 DA taxa (whisker plot, alpha-diversity scatter, and distance-to-centroid boxplots). Bottom-left column: (1) PERMDISP false positive rate sweep for Setting DA_no_sd (filtered), (2) PERMANOVA true positive rate sweep on the same Setting DA_no_sd simulations (filtered), and (3) FNR-FPR summary scatter. Bottom-right column: PCoA panels for Setting DA_no_sd (filtered)."
# Organize Figure 3 in two columns:
# Top row: whisker plot (40 DA) | alpha diversity (40 DA) | boxplots (40 DA)
# Bottom: Left column: 3 rows (Setting DA_no_sd filtered FPR sweep, Setting DA_no_sd PERMANOVA TPR sweep, scatter plot)
#         Right column: PCoA plots for Setting DA_no_sd filtered

# Check that all plots exist and are valid
cat("Checking plots for Figure 3...\n")
cat("  p_figure3_row1:", !is.null(p_figure3_row1), ", class:", class(p_figure3_row1), "\n")
cat("  p_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered_fpr:", !is.null(p_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered_fpr), ", class:", class(p_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered_fpr), "\n")
cat("  p_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_tpr:", !is.null(p_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_tpr), ", class:", class(p_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_tpr), "\n")
cat("  p_scatter_fnr_fpr:", !is.null(p_scatter_fnr_fpr), ", class:", class(p_scatter_fnr_fpr), "\n")
cat("  pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered:", !is.null(pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered), ", class:", class(pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered), "\n")

# Try building patchwork step by step
left_col <- p_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered_fpr / p_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_tpr / p_scatter_fnr_fpr
cat("  left_col created, class:", class(left_col), "\n")

right_col <- pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered
cat("  right_col created, class:", class(right_col), "\n")

# Align bottom row with proper spacing
bottom_row <- left_col | right_col
cat("  bottom_row created, class:", class(bottom_row), "\n")

p_figure3 <- p_figure3_row1 / bottom_row
cat("  p_figure3 created, class:", class(p_figure3), "\n")

p_figure3 <- p_figure3 +
  patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect", widths = c(1, 1), heights = c(1, 3)) &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"), legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'), legend.position = "bottom")

p_figure3
```

```{r figure-3-save-pdf}
#| echo: false
ggsave("Figure_3_BritoIL_2016.pdf", p_figure3, width = 183, height = 230, units = "mm")
```

```{r cleanup-after-figure-3}
# Release heavy Figure 3 objects before supplementary figure 3
rm(list = intersect(
  c(
    "p_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered_fpr", "p_setting_DA_no_sd_permanova_tpr", "p_scatter_fnr_fpr",
    "pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered", "p_figure3_row1", "left_col", "right_col",
    "bottom_row", "p_figure3", "cmp_40_da", "sim_40_da", "sim_40_da_filtered",
    "p_whisker_40_da", "alpha_plot_40_da", "p_boxplots_40_da", "distances_non_ibd_40_da",
    "d_whisker_40_da", "fpr_fig3_data", "fpr_fig3", "fnr_fig2", "scatter_data", "plot_data",
    "permanova_da_results", "permanova_da_tpr",
    "pred_data", "lm_fit"
  ),
  ls()
))
invisible(gc())
```

## Supplementary Figure 3: Setting DA_no_sd and Setting DA_sd

### Setting DA_no_sd: Differential abundance, no sd on overdispersion

This setting sweeps over the number of DA taxa (0, 1, 2, 3, ...) using slopes sampled from **significant DA taxa only** (credible interval excludes 0). Uses **k = 0 (no mean-OD association)** to ensure proper FPR testing at n_da_taxa = 0; with `sd_log_overdispersion = 0`, taxa also share the same baseline OD within and across groups at the null. This tests how method performance changes as we progressively add more DA taxa, starting from no DA (FPR test) to increasing numbers of DA taxa (power test).

```{r supp-fig-3, fig.width=16, fig.height=16}
sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
sweep_setting_DA_sd_res <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_sd_res", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
# Use available seeds (with n_reps_auc=2, we have seeds 12001 and 12002)
available_seeds_supp <- unique(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs$seed)
seed_1_da_supp <- available_seeds_supp[1]
seed_20_da_supp <- if(length(available_seeds_supp) >= 2) available_seeds_supp[2] else available_seeds_supp[1]
seed_40_da_supp <- if(length(available_seeds_supp) >= 2) available_seeds_supp[2] else available_seeds_supp[1]
idx_1_da <- get_sweep_index(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs, seed_1_da_supp, 1)
idx_20_da <- get_sweep_index(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs, seed_20_da_supp, 20)
idx_40_da <- get_sweep_index(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs, seed_40_da_supp, 40)

# Job tables are no longer needed after index selection
rm(list = intersect(c("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs", "sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs", "available_seeds_supp"), ls()))
invisible(gc())

# Format analysis results for plotting
analysis_DA_no_sd_1 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res[[idx_1_da]], case_label = "1 DA")
analysis_DA_no_sd_20 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res[[idx_20_da]], case_label = "20 DA")
analysis_DA_no_sd_40 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res[[idx_40_da]], case_label = "40 DA")

analysis_DA_sd_1 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(sweep_setting_DA_sd_res[[idx_1_da]], case_label = "1 DA")
analysis_DA_sd_20 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(sweep_setting_DA_sd_res[[idx_20_da]], case_label = "20 DA")
analysis_DA_sd_40 <- format_analysis_results_for_plotting(sweep_setting_DA_sd_res[[idx_40_da]], case_label = "40 DA")

# Large result lists are no longer needed once subset analyses are extracted
rm(list = intersect(c("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res", "sweep_setting_DA_sd_res", "idx_1_da", "idx_20_da", "idx_40_da"), ls()))
invisible(gc())

pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd <- plot_pcoa_grid_multiple_methods(
  analysis_list = list(analysis_DA_no_sd_1, analysis_DA_no_sd_20, analysis_DA_no_sd_40),
  n_da_labels = c("1", "20", "40")
)
pcoa_panel_setting_DA_sd <- plot_pcoa_grid_multiple_methods(
  analysis_list = list(analysis_DA_sd_1, analysis_DA_sd_20, analysis_DA_sd_40),
  n_da_labels = c("1", "20", "40")
)

# Keep only the final PCoA panel objects
rm(list = intersect(
  c(
    "analysis_DA_no_sd_1", "analysis_DA_no_sd_20", "analysis_DA_no_sd_40",
    "analysis_DA_sd_1", "analysis_DA_sd_20", "analysis_DA_sd_40"
  ),
  ls()
))
invisible(gc())

if (exists("fpr_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered", inherits = FALSE)) {
  fpr_setting_DA_no_sd <- fpr_setting_DA_no_sd_filtered
} else {
  fpr_setting_DA_no_sd <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_fpr", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")
}
fpr_setting_DA_sd <- targets::tar_read("sweep_setting_DA_sd_fpr", store = "_targets_BritoIL_2016")

p_setting_DA_no_sd <- plot_da_taxa_sweep_results(
  fpr_data = fpr_setting_DA_no_sd,
  setting_name = paste0("Setting DA_no_sd: Differential abundance, no sd on overdispersion (n=", n_samples, ", p=", n_taxa, 
                       ", c=", round(intercept_disp_DA_no_sd, 2), 
                       ", σ=", round(sigma_DA_no_sd, 5), 
                       ", k=", round(k_fixed_DA_no_sd, 3), ")"),
  sigma_val = sigma_DA_no_sd,
  intercept_disp_val = intercept_disp_DA_no_sd
)

p_setting_DA_sd <- plot_da_taxa_sweep_results(
  fpr_data = fpr_setting_DA_sd,
  setting_name = paste0("Setting DA_sd: Differential abundance, with sd on overdispersion (n=", n_samples, ", p=", n_taxa, 
                       ", c=", round(intercept_disp_DA_sd, 2), 
                       ", σ=", round(sigma_DA_sd, 5), 
                       ", k=", round(k_fixed_DA_sd, 3),
                       ", sd_log_od=", round(prec_sd_real, 3), ")"),
  sigma_val = sigma_DA_sd,
  intercept_disp_val = intercept_disp_DA_sd
)

# Keep only final sweep plot objects
rm(list = intersect(c("fpr_setting_DA_no_sd", "fpr_setting_DA_sd"), ls()))
invisible(gc())

p_setting_DA_no_sd_top <- (p_setting_DA_no_sd | pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd) + 
  patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect", widths = c(1, 1)) &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"), legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'), legend.position = "bottom")

p_setting_DA_sd_top <- (p_setting_DA_sd | pcoa_panel_setting_DA_sd) + 
  patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect", widths = c(1, 1)) &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"), legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'), legend.position = "bottom")

fig_supp_3 <- p_setting_DA_no_sd_top / p_setting_DA_sd_top +
  patchwork::plot_layout(guides = "collect", heights = c(1, 1)) &
  theme(plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"), legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'), legend.position = "bottom")

fig_supp_3
```

```{r supp-fig-3-save-pdf}
#| echo: false
ggsave("Supp_Figure_3_BritoIL_2016.pdf", fig_supp_3, width = 183, height = 230, units = "mm")
```

```{r cleanup-after-supp-figure-3}
# Final cleanup before summary tables/text
rm(list = intersect(
  c(
    "sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_jobs", "sweep_setting_DA_sd_jobs",
    "sweep_setting_DA_no_sd_res", "sweep_setting_DA_sd_res",
    "analysis_DA_no_sd_1", "analysis_DA_no_sd_20", "analysis_DA_no_sd_40",
    "analysis_DA_sd_1", "analysis_DA_sd_20", "analysis_DA_sd_40",
    "pcoa_panel_setting_DA_no_sd", "pcoa_panel_setting_DA_sd",
    "fpr_setting_DA_no_sd", "fpr_setting_DA_sd",
    "p_setting_DA_no_sd", "p_setting_DA_sd",
    "p_setting_DA_no_sd_top", "p_setting_DA_sd_top", "fig_supp_3"
  ),
  ls()
))
invisible(gc())
```


# Summary

## Parameter Summary Table

```{r parameter-summary}
# Targets use v_effect-derived dispersion (see sccomp_params); prec_* may be NA / not used.
fmt_num <- function(x, d = 4) {
  if (length(x) != 1L || is.na(x)) return("N/A")
  as.character(round(x, d))
}
fmt_rng <- function(x) {
  paste0("[", round(min(x), 2), ", ", round(max(x), 2), "]")
}
prec_slope_txt <- if (exists("prec_slope") && length(prec_slope) == 1L && !is.na(prec_slope)) {
  as.character(round(prec_slope, 4))
} else {
  "N/A (optional fit summary)"
}
prec_int_txt <- if (exists("prec_intercept") && length(prec_intercept) == 1L && !is.na(prec_intercept)) {
  as.character(round(prec_intercept, 4))
} else {
  "N/A (optional fit summary)"
}

param_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  Parameter = c(
    "Number of samples",
    "Number of taxa (genera)",
    "prec_slope (optional; from extract-dispersion-params chunk)",
    "k used in targets (sccomp_params$k_real)",
    "Mean-OD association (= -k)",
    "prec_intercept (optional fit summary)",
    "c from fit (= -prec_intercept); sim uses v_effect for taxon c",
    "Precision SD (prec_sd) from fit, if read",
    "Baseline intercept range",
    "Group effect (slope) range"
  ),
  `sccomp Value` = c(
    as.character(n_samples_real),
    as.character(n_taxa_real),
    prec_slope_txt,
    fmt_num(k_real),
    fmt_num(-k_real),
    prec_int_txt,
    fmt_num(c_real),
    fmt_num(prec_sd_real),
    fmt_rng(intercept_effects),
    fmt_rng(slope_effects)
  ),
  `Simulation Value` = c(
    as.character(n_samples),
    as.character(n_taxa),
    "N/A",
    fmt_num(k_realistic),
    fmt_num(-k_realistic),
    "N/A",
    fmt_num(intercept_disp_realistic),
    fmt_num(prec_sd_real),
    fmt_rng(mu_inv_softmax_base_realistic),
    fmt_rng(slope_realistic)
  )
)

knitr::kable(param_summary, caption = "Summary of HMP2 parameters used for realistic simulation")
```

## Key Findings

The simulation uses parameters extracted directly from the HMP2 microbiome dataset sccomp fit.

### Parameter Mapping (sccomp → Simulator)

**sccomp uses precision**: `log(precision) ≈ prec_intercept + prec_slope × log(mean)` (indexed `prec_intercept_1`, `prec_slope_1` when bimodal)

**Simulator uses overdispersion (σ)**: `log(σ) = -k × log(mean) + c`

Since `precision ≈ 1/σ`, we have `log(σ) ≈ -log(precision)`, so:

- **k = prec_slope** = `r round(k_real, 4)` (the slope transfers directly)
- **c = -prec_intercept** = `r round(c_real, 4)` (the intercept is negated)

### Interpretation

With k = `r round(k_real, 4)` (negative):
- The simulator formula becomes: `log(σ) = -(`r round(k_real, 4)`) × log(μ) + c = +`r round(-k_real, 4)` × log(μ) + c`
- **Higher mean abundance → higher σ → MORE overdispersion** ✓

This matches the biological expectation that abundant taxa tend to be more variable.

### Additional Parameters

- **Variability around k (prec_sd)**: `r round(prec_sd_real, 4)` - This adds realistic variation to the simulations.
- **Baseline abundances**: Sampled from the real HMP2 intercept distribution.
- **Group effects (slopes)**: Sampled from the real HMP2 IBD vs Healthy effect distribution.

4. **Baseline abundances**: Sampled from the real distribution of intercept effects, capturing the natural variation in taxa abundances.

5. **Group effects**: Based on the real IBD vs Healthy effect size distribution.

This approach ensures the simulation conditions reflect what we observe in real microbiome studies, providing more meaningful validation of beta-diversity distance inputs for PERMDISP.
